wines.name: Giuseppe Quintarelli Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 7-8 years in large Slavonian oak casks before release

taste.summary: The definitive benchmark Amarone: monumental concentration from multi-variety appassimento, extraordinary aromatic depth of dried fruit, leather and tobacco, with firm but resolved tannins and a finish of exceptional length.

pairings.dish: Braised beef short rib with polenta
pairings.why: The wine's dried-fruit concentration and firm tannins embrace the collagen richness of long-braised beef; polenta's neutrality lets the Amarone speak.
Crystalline salt crystals and umami amplify the wine's savoury-cedar register.
Earthy mushroom umami mirrors the wine's incense and tobacco complexity.

history.summary: Giuseppe Quintarelli began commercial bottling of his Amarone in the 1950s from the family's holdings above Negrar. He rejected any shortcuts in production: minimum four years drying on bamboo trellises in the fruttaio, seven-plus years in large Slavonian oak, and no filtration. The result is the longest-lived and most collected Amarone of the Classico zone, with the estate now run by his daughter Fiorenza.

milestones.event: First documented Quintarelli Amarone bottling
Extended maceration and long oak regime codified under Giuseppe's singular approach
Giuseppe Quintarelli passes away; Fiorenza Quintarelli takes over direction

wines.description: The benchmark Valpolicella Amarone Classico DOCG from Giuseppe Quintarelli, aged seven-plus years in large Slavonian oak. A wine of extraordinary concentration and complexity from old Corvina and indigenous varieties.

wines.name: Giuseppe Quintarelli Alzero
wines.oak_regime: Aged 7 years in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: Quintarelli's Bordeaux varieties treated to the Valpolicella appassimento method: dried Cabernet and Merlot producing extraordinary density, graphite-inflected fruit and a seamlessly long finish.

pairings.why: The Cabernet spine and graphite tannins are the perfect counterweight to charred Chianina beef fat.
Gamey, resinous stew finds its match in the wine's dried-herb and cedar notes.
Sharp, nutty northern Italian cheese sharpens the wine's dark fruit register.

history.summary: Quintarelli developed Alzero as a unique experiment applying the appassimento drying method to Bordeaux grape varieties, producing a Veneto IGT rather than DOCG as the rules did not accommodate the blend. It is one of Italy's rarest and most sought-after cult wines, produced in tiny quantities from the estate's highest plots.

milestones.event: First Alzero vintage, applying appassimento to Cabernet and Merlot
Alzero established as a critical benchmark among collectors

wines.description: Quintarelli's sui-generis Veneto IGT applying the appassimento drying process to Cabernet Franc, Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. One of Italy's rarest and most collectible bottles.

wines.name: Giuseppe Quintarelli Recioto della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 5-6 years in large Slavonian oak

taste.summary: The sweet passito counterpart to Quintarelli's Amarone: residual sugar balanced by firm tannins and high acidity producing a luscious yet structured dessert red with decades-long ageing potential.

pairings.why: The wine's chocolate and dried-cherry notes echo the cocoa bitterness.
Sweet Recioto bridges the creamy saltiness of Gorgonzola beautifully.
The wine's raisin and fig notes harmonise with the concentrated baked fruit.

history.summary: Recioto predates Amarone historically as the prestige wine of Valpolicella, using only the upper grape clusters (reci) with the highest concentration. Quintarelli's version is the gold standard, made in small quantity and aged with the same patience as his dry wines.

milestones.event: First documented Quintarelli Recioto bottling
Recioto della Valpolicella awarded DOCG status

wines.description: Giuseppe Quintarelli's Recioto della Valpolicella Classico DOCG, the sweet passito red made from the finest uppermost clusters of Corvina and Corvinone, aged six years in large casks.

wines.name: Giuseppe Quintarelli Valpolicella Classico Superiore
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: Quintarelli's entry-level Valpolicella is already a lesson in traditional winemaking: high-acid, savoury cherry fruit with dried-herb complexity and three years in large oak adding gentle structure.

pairings.why: The wine's tart cherry and herbal register cuts through the starchy, fatty bean and pasta broth.
Light tannins and sour cherry acidity lift the mild fat of grilled veal.
Regional charcuterie's fennel and spice notes harmonise with the wine's dried-herb character.

history.summary: Quintarelli's Superiore shows the producer's philosophy applied to the lighter Valpolicella DOC classification, with three years in large oak producing surprising complexity for what many consider a secondary label.

milestones.event: Valpolicella Superiore production established alongside Amarone

wines.description: Giuseppe Quintarelli's Valpolicella Classico Superiore DOC: a savoury, high-acid expression aged three years in Slavonian oak that stands far above its classification.

wines.name: Giuseppe Quintarelli Primofiore
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3-4 years in large Slavonian oak

taste.summary: A blend of local Corvina with international Cabernet in the Quintarelli style: concentrated, savoury and structured, with the Bordeaux varieties adding colour and spine to the Valpolicella base.

pairings.why: The wine's Cabernet tannins and dried-herb notes are a natural partner for slow-cooked lamb.
Local hard cheese with crystalline texture picks up the wine's savoury-fruit register.

history.summary: Primofiore represents Quintarelli's blend of indigenous Valpolicella varieties with Cabernet, declassified to Veneto IGT to allow the non-native grapes. Despite its IGT status it is one of the estate's most sought-after bottles among collectors.

milestones.event: First Primofiore vintage as Veneto IGT

wines.description: Quintarelli's Veneto IGT blending Corvina with Cabernet Sauvignon and Franc, aged in large oak. A collector's wine showcasing his unique approach to international varieties in the Valpolicella context.

wines.name: Dal Forno Romano Amarone della Valpolicella
wines.oak_regime: Aged 4 years in small French oak barriques (225L), followed by 1 year in bottle before release

taste.summary: The most powerful and extracted Amarone on the market: tiny yields, four-month drying, four years in small French oak. Immense concentration of dried fruit, dark chocolate and iron. Built for multi-decade cellaring.

pairings.dish: Braised beef cheeks with bone marrow
pairings.why: The wine's almost overwhelming concentration needs the fat and collagen richness of long-braised ox cheek.
Hard mountain cheese provides salt and umami contrast to the wine's dark fruit density.

pairings.dish: Dark chocolate truffle with sea salt
pairings.why: The wine's chocolate-espresso notes and bitter finish pair with high-cocoa dark chocolate.

history.summary: Romano Dal Forno began producing commercial Amarone in 1990 from his Illasi valley estate, outside the Classico zone. His approach applies Burgundy-style micro-management to Valpolicella: extremely low yields of 500g per vine, four months of drying in refrigerated fruttai, and barrique ageing. The result is Amarone of an entirely different register from the classical approach, attracting international critics and 100-point scores.

milestones.event: First Dal Forno Amarone commercial release
Dal Forno introduces refrigerated fruttai for controlled drying conditions
Marco Dal Forno joins his father in winery direction

wines.description: Dal Forno Romano's Amarone della Valpolicella DOCG from the Illasi valley: the most concentrated and powerful expression of the appellation, aged in French barriques for four years.

wines.name: Dal Forno Romano Valpolicella Superiore
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in small French oak barriques

taste.summary: Dal Forno's entry into the Valpolicella range already shows the estate's signature intensity: deeply coloured, barrique-aged and concentrated beyond what the DOC classification implies.

pairings.why: The wine's concentrated cherry-tobacco profile and firm tannins match the richness of slow-cooked duck.
Firm tannins and high acidity cut through the fat of grilled lamb while complementing its gamey notes.

history.summary: The Valpolicella Superiore was Romano Dal Forno's first commercial release and set the template for his subsequent wines: extreme concentration, barrique ageing, and minimal production. It is often cited as the best Valpolicella produced.

milestones.event: First Dal Forno Valpolicella Superiore release

wines.description: Dal Forno Romano's Valpolicella DOC made to Amarone intensity standards: low yields, barrique ageing and extraordinary concentration for the classification.

wines.name: Dal Forno Romano Recioto della Valpolicella
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3-4 years in small French oak barriques

taste.summary: Dal Forno's sweet Recioto applies the same barrique-and-concentration philosophy: deep, almost port-like sweetness balanced by the estate's signature tannin structure.

pairings.why: Dark chocolate and cherry liqueur notes in the wine echo the molten chocolate centre.
Intense blue cheese saltiness is tamed and elevated by the wine's lush sweetness.

history.summary: Dal Forno produces his Recioto in tiny quantities, extending his signature approach of extreme concentration and barrique ageing to the sweet red tradition of Valpolicella.

milestones.event: First Dal Forno Recioto release in small quantities

wines.description: Dal Forno Romano's sweet Recioto della Valpolicella DOCG: made in tiny quantities with the same barrique-and-concentration philosophy as his Amarone.

wines.name: Allegrini Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 4 years in large Slavonian oak and new French barriques (blend of sizes)

taste.summary: Allegrini's estate Amarone Classico blends Corvina and Corvinone in nearly equal measure with a small percentage of Oseleta; the result is a polished, internationally styled Amarone with plush dried-fruit concentration and a refined tannin frame.

pairings.why: The wine's dried-plum richness and firm tannins support long-braised beef as well as any Piedmontese red.
Crystalline umami of old Parmigiano amplifies the wine's savoury cedar finish.
The Oseleta component's peppery warmth aligns with earthy game and mushroom.

history.summary: Allegrini began producing Amarone in the mid-20th century, and under Giovanni Allegrini's leadership in the 1980s it became one of the first estates to bottle and market single-vineyard Amarone internationally. The estate Classico remains its most commercially visible wine and has helped define the modern, polished style of the appellation.

milestones.event: Early Allegrini Amarone production established
Giovanni Allegrini modernises winemaking with barrique integration
Marilisa Allegrini takes over estate direction after Giovanni's death

wines.description: Allegrini's estate Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG: a polished, internationally recognised expression blending Corvina and Corvinone with small doses of Oseleta.

wines.name: Allegrini Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Fieramonte
wines.oak_regime: Aged 4-5 years in a combination of large Slavonian casks and new French barriques

taste.summary: The single-vineyard Fieramonte takes Allegrini's Amarone to the highest level: a single hill block in the Classico zone with extreme concentration, the Oseleta component adding signature pepper warmth to the cedar-espresso complex.

pairings.dish: Venison loin with juniper and black truffle
pairings.why: The wine's iron-mineral spine and dried-fruit intensity embrace the gamey, earthy complexity of venison and truffle.
Long-aged Asiago's crystalline texture and nutty salt amplify the wine's concentration.

history.summary: Fieramonte was one of the first single-vineyard Amarone bottlings in the Classico zone, pioneered by Allegrini to demonstrate site-level expression within the appellation. The vineyard in Fumane faces south-east and benefits from optimal diurnal range for slow Corvina drying.

milestones.event: First Fieramonte single-vineyard Amarone release, among the earliest in Classico

wines.description: Allegrini's single-vineyard Amarone Classico DOCG from the Fieramonte hill in Fumane, one of the first single-cru Amarone bottlings in the appellation, showing extreme concentration and site specificity.

wines.name: Allegrini La Poja
wines.oak_regime: Aged 18-24 months in French barriques (225L)

taste.summary: A 100% Corvina IGT aged in French barriques rather than dried: La Poja demonstrates the varietal's structure and ageing potential when treated outside the appassimento framework, producing a powerful, savoury red that rivals Amarone in complexity.

pairings.why: Pure Corvina tannin and high acidity cut through the fat of a thick Florentine steak.
Wild boar ragu's gamey depth is complemented by the wine's leather-cedar register.

history.summary: La Poja was created by Giovanni Allegrini as a single-vineyard, non-appassimento Corvina to show that the grape could produce world-class still red wine without drying. Declassified to IGT to allow the unconventional approach, it became one of Italy's notable single-varietal experiments.

milestones.event: First La Poja vintage, one of Italy's earliest single-vineyard 100% Corvina IGT bottlings

wines.description: Allegrini's 100% Corvina Veronese IGT from a single vineyard: La Poja was among the first to demonstrate Corvina's power outside the appassimento tradition, aged in French barriques.

wines.name: Allegrini Palazzo della Torre
wines.oak_regime: Aged 12 months in French barriques; partial ripasso over dried Amarone pomace

taste.summary: A Veronese IGT using partial ripasso over Amarone pomace adding richness to the Corvina-Rondinella blend, while a small Sangiovese component adds acidity. Approachable but complex mid-tier red from Allegrini.

pairings.why: The wine's cherry-tobacco notes and medium body are a classic match for Veneto-style meat sauce.
Approachable tannins and good acidity work well with cured salami and prosciutto pizza.
Dried herbs and cherry fruit complement grilled lamb's mild gamey character.

history.summary: Palazzo della Torre was designed as Allegrini's accessible entry point into the ripasso-adjacent style, using a small percentage of late-harvested dried grapes to add complexity to the fresh Corvina base.

milestones.event: Palazzo della Torre launched as Allegrini's approachable mid-range Veronese IGT

wines.description: Allegrini's accessible Veronese IGT using partial ripasso over dried grape pomace; Corvina-Rondinella-Sangiovese blend with tobacco-cherry character and good value.

wines.name: Masi Campolongo di Torbe Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 4 years in large Slavonian oak 50HL casks

taste.summary: From the single Torbe hillside vineyard in the Negrar valley, Campolongo di Torbe is characterised by spicy, tobacco-inflected dried fruit with earthier notes than Masi's more opulent Mazzano bottling.

pairings.why: The earthier, tobacco-inflected style matches game stew's complexity.
The classic pairing for Amarone: reduction with the same wine creates flavour echo.
Local aged cheese from the Lessini hills provides complementary mineral depth.

history.summary: Campolongo di Torbe was one of the first single-vineyard Amarone designations released by Masi, in response to the producer's philosophy that the Classico zone's individual hills produce distinct expressions. The Torbe vineyard on calcareous soils in the Negrar valley produces a characteristically earthy, tobacco-driven style.

milestones.event: First Campolongo di Torbe single-vineyard Amarone release
Production shifted to strict single-vintage bottling from the Torbe hillside

wines.description: Masi's single-vineyard Amarone Classico DOCG from the Torbe hillside in the Negrar valley, first released in 1964. Earthy, tobacco-inflected style aged in large Slavonian casks.

verified.address_quoted: Via Monteleone, 37015 Gargagnago di Sant'Ambrogio di Valpolicella (VR)

wines.name: Masi Mazzano Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 4 years in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: Mazzano is Masi's most powerful and concentrated single-vineyard Amarone, from the high-altitude Mazzano hill in the S. Ambrogio zone. More opulent and riper than Campolongo, with a chocolatey, rich palate and remarkable ageing potential.

pairings.why: The wine's plum-chocolate opulence aligns with the rich, fruity sauce of roast duck.
The classic Veronese braised beef dish ideally uses Amarone in the braise, creating a flavour mirror.
The sharp, crystalline, long-aged Friulian cheese provides salt and umami contrast.

history.summary: Mazzano was released alongside Campolongo di Torbe as Masi's pair of single-vineyard Amarone expressions. The Mazzano vineyard, at higher elevation and on deeper volcanic soils in S. Ambrogio di Valpolicella, consistently produces Masi's richest, most concentrated wine and is considered one of the top single-vineyard Amarone bottlings in the Classico zone.

milestones.event: First Mazzano single-vineyard Amarone bottling released
Mazzano established as Masi's premium single-vineyard flagship

wines.description: Masi's flagship single-vineyard Amarone Classico DOCG from the high-altitude Mazzano hill in S. Ambrogio; opulent, chocolate-plum concentration with multi-decade ageing potential.

verified.address_quoted: Via Monteleone, 37015 Gargagnago di Sant'Ambrogio di Valpolicella (VR)

wines.name: Masi Costasera Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 2 years in large Slavonian oak casks followed by 1 year in Allier barriques

taste.summary: Masi's entry-level Classico Amarone is the house's most widely distributed wine and among the most accessible classic-zone bottlings, balancing dried-cherry concentration with a restrained herbaceous character and good structure.

pairings.why: The wine's cherry-tobacco notes and firm tannins are a textbook pairing with slow-braised Veronese-style beef.
Game richness is balanced by the wine's dried herb and cedar notes.
Salt crystals and umami of long-aged Grana amplify the wine's savoury tobacco register.

history.summary: Costasera was developed as Masi's Classico-zone entry Amarone, giving wider access to the appellation alongside the single-vineyard Mazzano and Campolongo. Named after the terraced slopes (costa sera means evening hillside) it draws from vineyards across the Classico zone.

milestones.event: First Costasera vintage as Masi's appellation-level Amarone Classico

wines.description: Masi Agricola's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG estate bottling; a widely distributed and well-structured expression of the Classico zone aged in Slavonian oak and Allier barriques.

verified.address_quoted: Via Monteleone, 37015 Gargagnago di Sant'Ambrogio di Valpolicella (VR)

wines.name: Masi Campofiorin Rosso del Veronese
wines.oak_regime: Aged 12-18 months in large Slavonian oak; ripasso over dried Amarone grape skins

taste.summary: Campofiorin pioneered the ripasso technique in 1964, passing fresh Valpolicella wine over dried Amarone grape skins to extract colour, richness and complexity. A seminal wine that created an entire style category.

pairings.why: Medium tannins and dried-herb notes complement cured meats and molten cheese beautifully.
The wine's medium body and cherry-tobacco notes pair seamlessly with meat lasagne.
A classic Venetian combination; the wine's approachable tannins work with fatty pork sausage.

history.summary: Campofiorin is arguably the wine that invented the ripasso technique as a commercial category. In 1964, Masi's Sandro Boscaini experimented with passing the new vintage's Valpolicella wine over the still-warm skins from the Amarone fermentation, extracting additional colour, sugar and complexity. The technique became the foundation of the modern Valpolicella Ripasso DOC designation.

milestones.event: First Campofiorin vintage establishes the ripasso technique
Campofiorin becomes Masi's most exported wine internationally
Valpolicella Ripasso DOC appellation formally created, largely due to Campofiorin's commercial success

wines.description: Masi's Campofiorin Rosso del Veronese IGT, the wine that pioneered the ripasso technique in 1964 by passing Valpolicella over dried Amarone skins. A seminal, widely accessible Italian red.

verified.address_quoted: Via Monteleone, 37015 Gargagnago di Sant'Ambrogio di Valpolicella (VR)

wines.name: Bertani Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged minimum 8 years in large Slavonian oak 50-100HL casks; often longer for special editions

taste.summary: Bertani's Amarone is the reference for the traditional, extremely long-aged style: minimum eight years in large Slavonian oak produces a garrigue-herb and leather-inflected wine of remarkable freshness and longevity, lighter in colour than modern Amarone but strikingly complex.

pairings.why: Beef braised in Amarone is the canonical pairing; Bertani's leather-herb notes reinforce the braise.
The savoury, nutty cheese matches the wine's herb-leather complexity.
Earthy porcini mushroom umami resonates with the wine's cedar and dried-herb character.

history.summary: Bertani has been producing Amarone from Negrar since the appellation's earliest commercial period. The estate's signature is extreme patience: minimum eight years in large casks is standard, giving wines that evolve very slowly and show a dried-herb, leather character quite distinct from modern fruit-forward styles. Bertani holds a large library of back vintages and regularly releases old vintages at cellar door.

milestones.event: Bertani winery founded by brothers Gaetano and Giovan Battista Bertani
Bertani Amarone commercial bottling begins
Bertani acquired by Tenimenti Angelini, traditional long-oak approach maintained

wines.description: Bertani's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG: aged a minimum of eight years in large Slavonian oak casks, one of the most traditional and long-lived expressions of the appellation.

wines.name: Bertani Valpolicella Ripasso
wines.oak_regime: Aged 18 months in large Slavonian oak; refermented on dried Amarone skins

taste.summary: Bertani's Ripasso benefits from the estate's traditional Corvina-Rondinella blend and refermentation over dried Amarone skins, producing a mid-weight red with the freshness typical of the traditional house style.

pairings.dish: Pasta e fasoi (bean and pasta soup)
pairings.why: The wine's high acidity and medium tannin complement the starchy, leguminous soup of the Veneto.
Cherry fruit and tobacco notes pair comfortably with charred, sweet pork ribs.

history.summary: Bertani's Valpolicella Ripasso was established as the estate's mid-tier offering, sitting between their fresh Valpolicella and the long-aged Amarone. The ripasso technique adds structure and dried-fruit character to the fresh Corvina base.

milestones.event: Bertani Valpolicella Ripasso first produced
Valpolicella Ripasso formally recognised as its own DOC appellation

wines.description: Bertani's Valpolicella Ripasso DOC: medium-weight, high-acid Corvina-Rondinella blend refermented over dried Amarone skins, aged 18 months in large Slavonian oak.

wines.name: Tommasi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Ca' Florian
wines.oak_regime: Aged 4 years in large Slavonian oak casks (2500L)

taste.summary: Ca' Florian is Tommasi's single-vineyard flagship Amarone from the old Ca' Florian hillside in the Classico zone, showing dense, chocolatey concentration with elegant tannin structure and long mineral finish.

pairings.why: The canonical Veronese pairing; Ca' Florian's chocolate-plum notes are ideal in the braise.
Firm tannins and dried herbs echo the rosemary crust; the wine's acidity balances lamb fat.

history.summary: Ca' Florian was created by Tommasi to express the specific character of their old Ca' Florian hillside vineyard in the Classico zone; a step up in concentration and ageing potential from the estate's standard Amarone Classico, released in small quantities.

milestones.event: First Ca' Florian single-vineyard Amarone release

wines.description: Tommasi's single-vineyard Ca' Florian Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG, aged four years in large Slavonian oak. The estate's prestige bottling from old vines in the Classico zone.

verified.address_quoted: Via Ronchetto 2, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Tommasi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: Tommasi's estate Amarone Classico represents the fourth-generation family's traditional approach: a high proportion of Corvinone adds floral lift to the dried-cherry concentration while three years in large casks develops structure.

pairings.dish: Beef stew with rosemary and garlic
pairings.why: The wine's firm tannins and dried herb notes integrate perfectly with long-simmered beef.
Local Verona mountain cheese brings salt and fat to balance the wine's concentration.

history.summary: Tommasi has been producing Amarone since the early years of the commercial appellation. The family's traditional approach with large Slavonian oak casks has remained consistent across four generations in the Classico zone.

milestones.event: Tommasi begins Amarone production from San Pietro in Cariano vineyards
Fourth-generation Giancarlo Tommasi takes over winemaking

wines.description: Tommasi's estate Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG, a traditional Corvina-Corvinone expression aged three years in large Slavonian casks from the family's San Pietro in Cariano vineyards.

verified.address_quoted: Via Ronchetto 2, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Tommasi Valpolicella Classico Superiore Ripasso
wines.oak_regime: Aged 18 months in Slavonian oak; refermented on dried Amarone skins

taste.summary: Tommasi's Ripasso offers the hallmark refermentation complexity over dried Amarone skins in a well-structured, cherry-tobacco dominated medium red that is one of the zone's best-value offerings in the Ripasso DOC.

pairings.why: Cherry-tobacco notes and medium tannins are classic Italian pasta-pairing territory.
Northern Italian pork sausage with fennel is a natural regional pairing with Valpolicella styles.
The wine's earthy tobacco component finds resonance with porcini or chanterelle mushrooms.

history.summary: Tommasi introduced their Ripasso as the mid-tier between their fresh Valpolicella and the Amarone, building on the zone's tradition of passing wine over the warm dried pomace from Amarone fermentation to add complexity.

milestones.event: Tommasi Valpolicella Ripasso first released

wines.description: Tommasi's Valpolicella Ripasso DOC: medium-bodied, cherry-tobacco Corvina-Corvinone blend refermented over dried Amarone skins with 18 months in Slavonian oak.

verified.address_quoted: Via Ronchetto 2, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Zenato Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian oak casks; followed by 1 year in small French oak barriques

taste.summary: Zenato's Classico Amarone incorporates 20% Oseleta adding firm pepper tannin and dark fruit spice to the Corvina-led blend; aged in a combination of large Slavonian and small French oak that bridges traditional and modern styles.

pairings.why: Gamey boar and buckwheat polenta demand the weight and spice the Oseleta provides.

pairings.dish: Braised beef cheeks with root vegetables
pairings.why: Long-braised collagen-rich beef aligns with the wine's dried-fruit concentration and tannin.

history.summary: Founded by Sergio Zenato, the estate began producing Classico Amarone in 1967 and was among the early producers to incorporate Oseleta into their blend, the native but often-overlooked variety that adds spice and colour depth. Now run by Alberto and Nadia Zenato.

milestones.event: First Zenato Amarone Classico vintage under Sergio Zenato
Alberto Zenato takes over winemaking direction

wines.description: Zenato's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG with 20% Oseleta adding spice and pepper depth, aged in both large Slavonian and small French oak.

verified.address_quoted: Via San Benedetto 8, 37019 Peschiera del Garda (VR)

wines.name: Speri Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Vigneto Monte Sant'Urbano
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian oak casks (20-50HL)

taste.summary: Monte Sant'Urbano is Speri's flagship single-vineyard Amarone from the historic cru on the Fumane hillside; traditional large-cask ageing preserves freshness alongside the dried-fruit concentration, producing one of the Classico zone's most elegant and age-worthy Amarone.

pairings.dish: Leg of venison with black truffle
pairings.why: The wine's savoury iron-cedar finish and high acidity are ideal with earthy game and truffle.
Crystalline umami of long-aged Parmigiano amplifies the mineral finish.

history.summary: Speri's Monte Sant'Urbano has been one of the Classico zone's recognised single-vineyard Amarone since its first release, reflecting the high-altitude Fumane hillside plot's capacity for fine, structured wine with exceptional freshness for the style.

milestones.event: First Monte Sant'Urbano single-vineyard Amarone release
Luca Speri takes over as winemaker, refining the traditional large-cask approach

wines.description: Speri's single-vineyard Monte Sant'Urbano Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG, from the historic Fumane hillside cru. Traditional large-cask ageing delivering elegance and exceptional freshness.

verified.address_quoted: Via Fontana 14, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Speri Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: Speri's estate Amarone Classico reflects the six-generation family's traditional approach with large-cask ageing, producing a fresh, floral, tobacco-inflected Amarone that remains one of the most consistent classic-zone expressions.

pairings.why: Wild boar ragu's fatty richness is balanced by the wine's high acidity and dried-herb character.
Local mountain cheese from the Lessini hills is the classic regional pairing.

history.summary: Speri has been farming the Monte Masua and Sant'Urbano hillsides since the 19th century and bottling Amarone since the appellation's commercial origins. The estate's philosophy is traditional large-cask ageing preserving the natural acidity and floral freshness of Corvina.

milestones.event: Speri Amarone estate bottling established
Speri family vineyard origins on the hillsides of Fumane

wines.description: Speri's estate Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG, aged in large Slavonian casks. A traditional, tobacco-inflected expression from the six-generation Fumane family estate.

verified.address_quoted: Via Fontana 14, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Tedeschi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Capitel Monte Olmi
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3-4 years in large Slavonian oak casks and small French oak barriques (mixed)

taste.summary: Capitel Monte Olmi is Tedeschi's single-vineyard prestige Amarone, from the Monte Olmi hill above San Pietro in Cariano, with exceptional concentration and finesse, reflecting one of the oldest family estates in Valpolicella.

pairings.why: The wine's iron minerality and dark chocolate note match the sweet-sour tomato braise.
Nutty, crystalline Grana provides umami contrast to the wine's dried-fruit density.

history.summary: Tedeschi has farmed the Monte Olmi vineyard since the 19th century, and their single-vineyard Amarone from this cru has been one of the appellation's most acclaimed since the 1970s. The six-generation family estate remains one of the oldest continuous producers in Valpolicella Classico.

milestones.event: First Capitel Monte Olmi single-vineyard Amarone release
Tedeschi family vineyard origins in Valpolicella Classico

wines.description: Tedeschi's single-vineyard flagship Amarone Classico DOCG from the Monte Olmi hillside cru, aged in Slavonian and French oak. Among the oldest continuously produced Amarone single-cru bottlings.

verified.address_quoted: Via G. Verdi 4, 37020 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Tedeschi Valpolicella Classico Superiore Capitel dei Nicalo
wines.oak_regime: Aged 18 months in Slavonian oak casks; partial ripasso over dried grape skins

taste.summary: The Capitel dei Nicalo is Tedeschi's Valpolicella Superiore partially ripassoed over dried skins, offering accessible Corvina cherry fruit with tobacco structure and good food-pairing versatility.

pairings.why: The classic Veronese red wine and meat sauce pairing; the wine's acidity cuts through the fat.
Fatty, savoury sausage is complemented by the wine's cherry fruit and medium tannin.

history.summary: Capitel dei Nicalo was developed as Tedeschi's accessible, food-friendly Valpolicella Superiore with partial ripasso adding complexity to the fresh Corvina blend.

milestones.event: First Capitel dei Nicalo Valpolicella Superiore release

wines.description: Tedeschi's Valpolicella Classico Superiore DOC with partial ripasso; a versatile, cherry-tobacco, medium-bodied red from the historic Valpolicella Classico family estate.

verified.address_quoted: Via G. Verdi 4, 37020 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Tenuta Sant'Antonio Amarone della Valpolicella Campo dei Gigli
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian oak casks and French barriques

taste.summary: The 20% Oseleta in Campo dei Gigli adds a peppery, dark-spice dimension to the Corvina-Corvinone dried fruit base; from the Mezzane valley outside the Classico zone, the wine shows an earthier, more structured style than comparable Classico Amarone.

pairings.dish: Wild boar with juniper and rosemary
pairings.why: Peppery Oseleta and earthy tobacco in the wine match gamey, resinous juniper boar perfectly.
The most aged Asiago's sharp crystalline texture cuts through the wine's density.

history.summary: The Castagnedi brothers established Campo dei Gigli as the single-vineyard flagship from their most prized parcel in the Mezzane valley, planting significant Oseleta to add complexity alongside the Corvina dominant in this non-Classico zone.

milestones.event: First Campo dei Gigli Amarone from the Mezzane single vineyard

wines.description: Tenuta Sant'Antonio's single-vineyard Campo dei Gigli Amarone DOCG from the Mezzane valley, with 20% Oseleta adding peppery spice and dark fruit complexity.

wines.name: Tenuta Sant'Antonio Valpolicella Superiore Ripasso
wines.oak_regime: Aged 18 months in Slavonian and French oak; refermented on Amarone dried skins

taste.summary: Tenuta Sant'Antonio's Ripasso shows the Mezzane valley's characteristic earthiness alongside cherry-tobacco fruit; the small Oseleta addition adds a distinguishing spice note that sets it apart from Classico-zone examples.

pairings.why: The wine's high acidity and medium tannin are perfect for the Venetian bean and pasta classic.
Herb-spiced chicken finds a match in the wine's tobacco and dried herb character.

history.summary: The Castagnedi brothers introduced their Valpolicella Ripasso as the estate's accessible tier, using the ripasso technique to bridge the fresh Valpolicella and the flagship Campo dei Gigli Amarone.

milestones.event: Tenuta Sant'Antonio Valpolicella Ripasso first release

wines.description: Tenuta Sant'Antonio's Valpolicella Ripasso DOC from the Mezzane valley; medium-bodied cherry-tobacco red with a peppery Oseleta edge, refermented over dried skins.

wines.name: Lorenzo Begali Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Monte Ca' Bianca
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: Begali's Monte Ca' Bianca is a traditional-school Amarone from very old Corvina vines; the deep mineral iron note and high acidity set it apart, making it one of the most distinctive and food-friendly examples in the Classico zone.

pairings.why: The wine's iron mineral character and high acidity find a perfect foil in gamey venison and polenta.
The local aged cheese from the Lessini hills is the quintessential regional pairing.

history.summary: Lorenzo and Giordana Begali founded the estate in 1986, specifically seeking out old Corvina vines on the Ca' Bianca hillside in San Pietro in Cariano. The old-vine concentration combined with traditional large-cask ageing produces a wine of notable depth and mineral distinctiveness.

milestones.event: First Monte Ca' Bianca Amarone from old Corvina vines at founding of estate

wines.description: Lorenzo Begali's Monte Ca' Bianca Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG, from very old Corvina vines, showing deep iron-mineral character and traditional large-cask freshness.

verified.address_quoted: Via Cengia 10, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Lorenzo Begali Recioto della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 2 years in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: Begali's Recioto is made from the same Ca' Bianca old-vine fruit as the Amarone, with the fermentation arrested before dryness; the result is a luscious, chocolate-cherry sweet red with firm tannic structure.

pairings.dish: Dark chocolate cake with cherry compote
pairings.why: The wine's chocolate-cherry character mirrors the dessert both in fruit and in bitter cocoa notes.
Sweet Recioto with salty, pungent blue cheese is a celebrated Veronese pairing.

history.summary: Begali makes Recioto in very small quantities from the best upper clusters of Ca' Bianca, maintaining the ancient tradition of sweet red wine in Valpolicella that predates the dry Amarone style.

milestones.event: First Begali Recioto Classico release

wines.description: Lorenzo Begali's Recioto della Valpolicella Classico DOCG, sweet passito red from old Ca' Bianca Corvina vines; made in tiny quantities with chocolate-cherry concentration.

verified.address_quoted: Via Cengia 10, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Zyme Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Kairos
wines.oak_regime: Aged 2 years in French barriques (225L) and 2 years in larger French tonneaux

taste.summary: Zyme's Kairos is a modern interpretation of Classico Amarone; former Quintarelli cellarmaster Celestino Gaspari applies small French oak ageing to produce a polished, concentrated expression that bridges traditional intensity with contemporary oak integration.

pairings.dish: Brasato with porcini mushrooms and polenta
pairings.why: The wine's espresso-cedar profile with firm tannin is a natural match for rich braised beef and mushrooms.
Crystalline salt and umami of Parmigiano amplify the wine's concentrated fruit.

history.summary: Celestino Gaspari founded Zyme in 2001 after years as cellarmaster at Quintarelli. Kairos was developed from the first vintage as the estate's flagship expression of Classico Amarone, applying Gaspari's experience of extreme appassimento to a smaller French oak ageing regime.

milestones.event: Zyme founded by Celestino Gaspari
First Kairos Amarone Classico release

wines.description: Zyme's flagship Kairos Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG, made by ex-Quintarelli cellarmaster Celestino Gaspari using small French oak ageing for a polished, modern-traditional expression.

verified.address_quoted: Via Ca' del Pipa 1, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Zyme Harlequin Veronese IGT
wines.oak_regime: Aged 24 months in a combination of French barriques and larger casks

taste.summary: A 15-variety blend in the Veronese IGT classification, Harlequin is Gaspari's conceptual statement: every grape variety grown across Zyme's vineyards co-fermented after partial drying, producing a complex, layered wine with extraordinary aromatic diversity.

pairings.dish: Braised wild boar with truffle and root vegetable
pairings.why: The wine's multi-variety complexity and high tannin demand the richest possible game dish.
Mountain hard cheese with good salinity balances the wine's dense concentration.

history.summary: Harlequin is Zyme's conceptual blend, representing Celestino Gaspari's artistic approach: all 15 grape varieties grown on the estate are harvested, partially dried and co-fermented. It is a unique and radical departure from normal DOC and IGT blending conventions.

milestones.event: First Harlequin Veronese IGT release as 15-variety co-ferment

wines.description: Zyme's conceptual 15-variety Veronese IGT: all estate grape varieties co-fermented after partial drying; an ambitious, complex and unique expression of Valpolicella's varietal diversity.

verified.address_quoted: Via Ca' del Pipa 1, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Brigaldara Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 2 years in large Slavonian oak casks and 1 year in French tonneaux (500L)

taste.summary: Brigaldara's Classico Amarone is known for its precise, traditional approach: careful appassimento in the estate's fruttai and mixed Slavonian/French oak ageing producing a structured, age-worthy expression.

pairings.why: The wine's dried-cherry concentration and firm tannins make it a natural match for the Veronese braised beef classic.
The local aged cheese in a risotto echoes the wine's cedar-mineral register.

history.summary: The Cesari family produces Amarone from the Classico zone vineyards around the Via Brigaldara estate in San Pietro in Cariano. A meticulous approach to appassimento has made Brigaldara a consistent quality benchmark.

milestones.event: Brigaldara estate established as a producing Valpolicella Classico estate in San Pietro in Cariano
First Brigaldara Amarone Classico release

wines.description: Brigaldara's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG, produced with precision from traditional fruttai drying and mixed Slavonian and French oak ageing.

verified.address_quoted: Via Brigaldara 20, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Ca' La Bionda Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: Ca' La Bionda's Amarone reflects the volcanic and calcareous soils of the Marano valley top: a mineral, iron-inflected style with high acidity and a savoury character quite distinct from estates lower in the valley.

pairings.dish: Lamb chops with rosemary and olive oil
pairings.why: The wine's high acidity and mineral spine cut cleanly through grilled lamb fat.
The local mountain cheese from the Lessini hills is the quintessential regional pairing for Valpolicella wine.

history.summary: The Castellani family has farmed the upper Marano valley since 1902, producing Amarone and Valpolicella from volcanic and calcareous soils at elevation that confer distinctive mineral freshness to their wines.

milestones.event: Ca' La Bionda estate established by the Castellani family
First commercial Amarone Classico bottling

wines.description: Ca' La Bionda's traditional Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG from the upper Marano valley; mineral, iron-inflected and high-acid from volcanic and calcareous soils.

wines.name: Ca' La Bionda Valpolicella Classico Superiore
wines.oak_regime: Aged 18 months in large Slavonian oak

taste.summary: Ca' La Bionda's Classico Superiore reflects the upper Marano valley's mineral character in a lighter-bodied, tart cherry register; crisp, food-friendly and genuinely typical of the traditional Valpolicella style.

pairings.why: The wine's high acidity and mineral freshness bridge red-sauce clam pasta beautifully.
Cherry fruit and mineral freshness complement the silky sweetness of prosciutto.

history.summary: Ca' La Bionda's Classico Superiore is the estate's everyday expression, emphasising the freshness and mineral character of the upper Marano valley soils.

milestones.event: First Ca' La Bionda Valpolicella Classico Superiore release

wines.description: Ca' La Bionda's Valpolicella Classico Superiore DOC: a traditional, high-acid, mineral-inflected cherry red from the volcanic upper Marano valley.

wines.name: Corte Sant'Alda Amarone della Valpolicella Mithas
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian and Allier oak casks; no filtration

taste.summary: Made by Demeter-certified biodynamic winemaker Marinella Camerani, Mithas is among the most distinctive Amarone: Demeter-certified estate viticulture produces a wine of unusual earthy complexity, mineral precision and high acidity that evolves beautifully with bottle age.

pairings.dish: Braised beef with wild mushroom and polenta
pairings.why: The wine's earthy mineral character and high acidity are a natural match for mushroom-braised beef.
Truffle's earthiness and the local cheese's salt resonate with the wine's clay-mineral register.

history.summary: Marinella Camerani founded Corte Sant'Alda in 1986 and converted to biodynamic viticulture, obtaining Demeter certification in 2003. Mithas (from the Latin for something precious) was developed as the estate's flagship Amarone, reflecting the Mezzane valley's calcareous-volcanic terroir and Camerani's minimal-intervention philosophy.

milestones.event: First Mithas Amarone release from Corte Sant'Alda
Corte Sant'Alda obtains Demeter biodynamic certification

wines.description: Corte Sant'Alda's Demeter-certified biodynamic Mithas Amarone della Valpolicella DOCG, from the Mezzane valley; earthy, mineral precision and exceptional acidity from Marinella Camerani's minimal-intervention approach.

wines.name: Corte Sant'Alda Soraigo Valpolicella Superiore
wines.oak_regime: Aged 12 months in large Slavonian oak; biodynamic production, no additives

taste.summary: Soraigo shows what biodynamic Valpolicella Classico tastes like with minimal intervention: earthy, mineral, tart cherry with clay soil expressiveness and a savoury, food-friendly character that makes it a superb natural-wine discovery.

pairings.why: The wine's high acidity and earthy mineral character complement the fatty, starchy richness of cotechino and lentils.
The wine's saline mineral quality and acidity bridge freshwater fish and brine perfectly.

history.summary: Soraigo was developed as Corte Sant'Alda's food-friendly everyday Valpolicella Superiore, made entirely without additions and with minimal intervention under Marinella Camerani's biodynamic philosophy.

milestones.event: First Soraigo Valpolicella Superiore release

wines.description: Corte Sant'Alda's Soraigo Valpolicella Superiore DOC, a Demeter-certified biodynamic, no-addition wine from the Mezzane valley showing earthy mineral freshness.

wines.name: Pieropan Soave Classico
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel fermentation and ageing; no oak

taste.summary: Pieropan's estate Soave Classico is the reference-standard expression of the appellation: Garganega's characteristic almond-blossom and white-peach aromas with a saline, mineral finish from the volcanic basalt hillside soils of the Classico zone.

pairings.dish: Pasta al burro e salvia (butter and sage pasta)
pairings.why: The wine's delicate almond-blossom note and high acidity lift the richness of sage-butter sauce.

pairings.dish: Grilled sea bass with capers and lemon
pairings.why: Saline mineral Soave is a benchmark white fish pairing; acidity balances the brine.
Seafood risotto's creamy, briny character is perfectly framed by Soave's almond acidity.

history.summary: Pieropan is the estate most responsible for rescuing Soave from its mid-20th-century commercial decline, when high-yield plain-grown Trebbiano diluted the appellation's reputation. Leonildo Pieropan insisted on hillside-only Garganega viticulture and single-vineyard bottlings, producing wines that demonstrated what Soave could achieve. The estate Soave Classico remains the foundation of that legacy.

milestones.event: Pieropan estate founded in the village of Soave
Leonildo Pieropan introduces single-vineyard Soave bottlings
Andrea and Dario Pieropan take over estate management

wines.description: Pieropan's estate Soave Classico DOC, the reference-standard expression of the appellation: almond-blossom Garganega with saline mineral finish from Classico hillside basalt soils.

wines.name: Pieropan Soave Classico Calvarino
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel fermentation; brief ageing on fine lees in stainless steel

taste.summary: Calvarino is Pieropan's basalt and limestone single-vineyard Soave, planted in 1924 to Garganega and Trebbiano di Soave; the volcanic basalt soils give a crackling acidity and mineral depth far beyond the appellation average, with exceptional ageing potential.

pairings.why: Sea bass poached in white wine and tomato water is the benchmark dish for mineral Soave.

pairings.dish: Seafood pasta with clams and bottarga
pairings.why: The wine's racy acidity and saline minerality amplify the iodine-sea-salt notes of bottarga.

pairings.dish: Asparagus di Bassano with poached egg
pairings.why: The Classico zone's own vegetable crop pairs perfectly with this mineral white; egg adds richness.

history.summary: Calvarino was the first single-vineyard Soave bottled by Pieropan, in 1971, establishing the concept that Soave hill sites could produce characterful terroir-driven wines. The plot was planted by Leonildo Pieropan on a basalt-over-limestone slope; the 1924 vines still contribute to the blend.

milestones.event: Calvarino vineyard originally planted with Garganega and Trebbiano di Soave
First Calvarino single-vineyard Soave Classico release; the first cru Soave in the modern era

wines.description: Pieropan's Calvarino single-vineyard Soave Classico DOC, the first cru Soave of the modern era, from 1924-planted Garganega vines on basalt-limestone soils with racy acidity and exceptional ageing potential.

wines.name: Pieropan Soave Classico La Rocca
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged 8 months in small French oak barriques; first introduced barriques to Soave

taste.summary: La Rocca is 100% Garganega from the volcanic tufa hillside above Soave castle, barrel-fermented and aged; arguably Italy's most cellar-worthy white wine, developing hazelnut-honey complexity over 10-plus years while retaining the grape's almond-mineral core.

pairings.why: White truffle's intense mineral-honey character finds a perfect complement in La Rocca's hazelnut-cream register.
Rich crustacean fat and the wine's golden apple-hazelnut weight are a classic luxury pairing.

pairings.dish: Veal carpaccio with aged Monte Veronese
pairings.why: The wine's oak weight and almond mineral backbone bridge the delicate veal and hard cheese.

history.summary: La Rocca was the first barrel-aged Soave, introducing French oak to the appellation in 1978. Named for the volcanic tufa hillside below Soave castle, the single-vineyard Garganega plot produces a wine of exceptional density and ageing capacity, demonstrating that Garganega on volcanic soil is one of Italy's greatest white wine grapes.

milestones.event: First La Rocca single-vineyard barrel-fermented Soave, pioneering oak in the appellation

wines.description: Pieropan's La Rocca single-vineyard 100% Garganega Soave Classico DOC, barrel-fermented from the volcanic tufa hillside; Italy's most age-worthy Garganega white, developing hazelnut-honey complexity over a decade.

wines.name: Inama Soave Classico Superiore Vigneti di Foscarino
wines.oak_regime: Fermented in French oak barriques; aged 8 months on fine lees in wood

taste.summary: Vigneti di Foscarino is Inama's pure basalt-volcanic Soave Classico from the historic Foscarino hill, the most volcanically active terroir in the Classico zone; 100% Garganega barrel-fermented for a mineral, almond-hazelnut complexity of striking length.

pairings.dish: Pasta with clam sauce (spaghetti alle vongole)
pairings.why: The wine's saline mineral character and racy acidity are the definitive match for Venetian clam pasta.
Asparagus from the Veneto plains finds a natural complement in the wine's almond and herbal notes.
The volcanic mineral salinity in the wine echoes the oceanic iodine of fresh oysters.

history.summary: Giuseppe Inama pioneered quality Soave Classico production from the Foscarino hill after acquiring the estate in 1991, recognising the extraordinary volcanic basalt soils as capable of producing world-class white wine. The Vigneti di Foscarino bottling established Inama's reputation as a Soave reference.

milestones.event: Inama founded; first Vigneti di Foscarino Soave Superiore DOCG
Matteo Inama takes over winemaking; refines barrel-fermentation approach

wines.description: Inama's Vigneti di Foscarino Soave Superiore DOCG, 100% Garganega barrel-fermented from the volcanic basalt Foscarino hill; saline mineral precision with racy acidity and good ageing potential.

wines.name: Inama Bradisismo Soave Superiore DOCG
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged 18-24 months in French oak barriques; a prestige selection from oldest vines

taste.summary: Bradisismo (Italian for seismic land subsidence) is Inama's prestige selection from the oldest basalt-grown Garganega vines, aged 18-24 months in French barriques; arguably the most complex Soave available, with extraordinary tension between rich oak ageing and the grape's saline mineral core.

pairings.why: The wine's richness and saline mineral backbone perfectly anchor the luxury of lobster and saffron risotto.
White truffle's mineral-honey character harmonises with Bradisismo's hazelnut-volcanic register.

history.summary: Bradisismo was created as Inama's top-tier single-selection Garganega from the oldest volcanic-soil vines in the Foscarino area, extended oak ageing pushing the wine into luxury white wine territory. The name references the slow volcanic ground movement that characterises the Soave Classico subsoil.

milestones.event: First Bradisismo prestige Soave Superiore DOCG release

wines.description: Inama's Bradisismo Soave Superiore DOCG, prestige selection from oldest volcanic basalt Garganega vines, aged 18-24 months in French barriques; the most complex and age-worthy white in the Soave appellation.

wines.name: Gini Soave Classico La Frosca
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel and large oak cask; aged on fine lees 6 months

taste.summary: La Frosca from the Gini family's oldest Garganega vines on the highest basalt-volcanic slopes of Monteforte d'Alpone; the chalky mineral texture, fennel herb and white-peach fruit represent the Classico zone's basalt terroir in its purest expression.

pairings.dish: Baccala alla vicentina (dried salt cod in milk)
pairings.why: The wine's saline mineral character and high acidity cut through the fat of Venetian salt cod.
Sweet scallop and citrus butter find the perfect counterpoint in almond-mineral Soave.

history.summary: The Gini family has farmed the basalt hillsides of Monteforte d'Alpone since the 17th century, and La Frosca was established as the single-vineyard expression from their highest and oldest Garganega vines. It is one of the Classico zone's most consistent and mineral-driven Soave bottlings.

milestones.event: Gini family establishes vineyard presence in Monteforte d'Alpone
First La Frosca single-vineyard Soave Classico release

wines.description: Gini's La Frosca single-vineyard 100% Garganega Soave Classico DOC from the volcanic basalt hillsides of Monteforte d'Alpone; chalky mineral texture with almond-blossom and white peach character.

wines.name: Gini Col Foscarin Soave Classico
wines.oak_regime: Partially barrel-fermented in French oak barriques; aged on lees 10 months

taste.summary: Col Foscarin is Gini's barrel-aged prestige Soave from the Col Foscarin parcel in Monteforte: partial barrel-fermentation adds hazelnut cream and golden apple richness while the Garganega's mineral core remains crisp and long-finishing.

pairings.dish: Risotto with asparagus and aged goat cheese
pairings.why: The wine's almond-hazelnut cream weight pairs beautifully with creamy risotto and tangy cheese.

pairings.dish: Seared tuna with black sesame and soy
pairings.why: The wine's nutty-mineral depth and weight complement the umami richness of seared tuna.

history.summary: Col Foscarin was Gini's innovation in barrel-aged Soave Superiore, taking the mineral Garganega fruit from their Col Foscarin parcel and adding richness through partial barrel-fermentation while maintaining the volcanic-soil mineral core.

milestones.event: First Col Foscarin Soave Superiore DOCG barrel-aged release from Gini

wines.description: Gini's Col Foscarin Soave Superiore DOCG, partially barrel-fermented 100% Garganega from the historic parcel in Monteforte d'Alpone; almond-hazelnut richness with saline volcanic mineral backbone.

wines.name: Suavia Soave Classico Monte Carbonare
wines.oak_regime: Fermented in stainless steel; aged 10 months on fine lees in large oak casks

taste.summary: Suavia's Monte Carbonare from the village of Soave itself: 100% Garganega from a steep volcanic tufa hillside with century-old vines; lees-aged in large casks producing exceptional mineral depth, citrus-almond precision and great cellaring potential.

pairings.dish: Crab with olive oil and lemon
pairings.why: The wine's saline mineral-umami character is the ideal foil for sweet crab and citrus.

pairings.dish: Tagliatelle al burro di noci (walnut butter pasta)
pairings.why: The wine's almond-citrus palate and racy acidity cut through rich walnut butter while complementing the nut.

history.summary: Suavia's Monte Carbonare was established as the estate's single-vineyard flagship from the Monte Carbonare hill directly above the village of Soave, where some of the oldest Garganega vines on volcanic tufa soil produce the appellation's most concentrated and mineral expressions.

milestones.event: First Monte Carbonare single-vineyard Soave Superiore DOCG release

wines.description: Suavia's Monte Carbonare single-vineyard 100% Garganega Soave Superiore DOCG from century-old vines on volcanic tufa; racy acidity, saline mineral depth and exceptional ageing potential.

wines.name: Suavia Soave Classico
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel fermentation and ageing; no oak

taste.summary: Suavia's estate Soave Classico is a fresh, mineral, everyday expression of Garganega from the village of Soave; classic almond-blossom aromatics with a crisp, saline finish perfect for aperitivo or seafood.

pairings.why: The wine's saline mineral freshness and acidity complement oily fish and citrus.
Light, crisp Soave cuts through the batter while matching the sea-salt of fried seafood.

history.summary: Suavia was founded in 1982 by the Tessari family in the village of Soave itself; the estate Classico is their workhorse expression of Garganega from the village's volcanic hillsides.

milestones.event: Suavia founded by the Tessari family; first estate Soave Classico DOC

wines.description: Suavia's estate Soave Classico DOC, fresh almond-blossom Garganega with saline mineral finish from the volcanic hillsides of the Soave village.

wines.name: Pra Monte Grande Soave Classico
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel fermentation; aged 10 months on fine lees in stainless steel tanks

taste.summary: Pra's Monte Grande from the basalt-rich Monteforte hillside is a benchmark organic Soave Classico: Graziano Pra's organic viticulture and extended lees contact produce a pure, mineral, textural wine of genuine complexity and age-worthiness for the price.

pairings.dish: Seppie in nero con polenta (black cuttlefish with polenta)
pairings.why: The wine's saline mineral depth is the classic pairing for cuttlefish ink braise and polenta.
The wine's almond-herbal character and high acidity complement the subtle bitterness of green asparagus risotto.

history.summary: Graziano Pra founded his estate in 1983 and was among the first Soave producers to convert to organic viticulture, seeking to maximise the volcanic basalt terroir's mineral expression without chemical interference. The Monte Grande vineyard on the Monteforte hillside is the estate's flagship plot.

milestones.event: Pra estate founded; first Monte Grande Soave Classico bottling
Pra estate converts to certified organic viticulture

wines.description: Pra's Monte Grande Soave Classico DOC, certified organic 100% Garganega from volcanic basalt hillside at Monteforte d'Alpone; mineral, chalky texture with racy acidity.

wines.name: Pra Otto Soave Classico
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged 14 months in French oak barriques (225L) on fine lees

taste.summary: Otto is Pra's barrel-aged prestige Soave, named for the year the oldest vines were planted (1908); extensive lees contact in barriques produces a Garganega of remarkable richness, hazelnut-cream complexity and long ageing potential.

pairings.why: The wine's hazelnut-cream depth and umami-like weight are a natural match for earthy porcini risotto.
Rich flat fish and butter sauce demand the full-weight Garganega treatment that Otto provides.

history.summary: Otto was created as Pra's top-tier barrel-aged expression from vines planted in 1908 (otto means eight in Italian, referencing 1908). Extended lees contact in French barriques adds complexity while the basalt soils maintain the mineral core.

milestones.event: First Otto Soave Classico barrel-aged release from Pra
Original vineyard planting that gives Otto its name

wines.description: Pra's Otto Soave Classico, barrel-fermented from 1908-planted Garganega vines on basalt at Monteforte d'Alpone; hazelnut-cream richness from 14 months in French barriques.

wines.name: Cantina di Soave Rocca Sveva Soave Classico Superiore
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel fermentation; aged 6 months on fine lees

taste.summary: Rocca Sveva is the flagship quality expression from the Cantina di Soave cooperative; a consistent, well-made Garganega-dominant Superiore DOCG that provides excellent value access to the appellation's almond-mineral character.

pairings.why: Crisp, light Soave acidity cuts through the batter and balances the garlic richness of aioli.
Almond freshness and acidity complement the tangy, creamy nature of fresh goat cheese.

history.summary: Rocca Sveva was developed as the Cantina di Soave cooperative's premium quality tier, representing the best hill-grown Garganega from the cooperative's 2,600 member growers. It provides wide accessibility to well-made Soave Classico at cooperative price points.

milestones.event: Cantina di Soave cooperative founded
Rocca Sveva label established as the cooperative's quality tier

wines.description: Cantina di Soave's Rocca Sveva Soave Superiore DOCG, the cooperative's quality flagship representing almond-blossom Garganega from Classico zone hillside growers at accessible price.

wines.name: Roberto Anselmi San Vincenzo
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel fermentation; no oak

taste.summary: San Vincenzo is Anselmi's most widely distributed wine, a Garganega-Chardonnay Veneto IGT that captures the almond-blossom freshness of the Soave zone in a light, food-friendly, affordable white. The Chardonnay adds body without altering the Garganega character.

pairings.why: The wine's crisp acidity and light mineral character are the ideal Venice-style table wine for fried seafood.
Salt cod cream on polenta is a Venetian classic; the wine's freshness balances the richness.

history.summary: San Vincenzo was named after the church overlooking Anselmi's Monteforte d'Alpone vineyards. Roberto Anselmi resigned from the Soave Consorzio in 2000 and declassified all his wines to Veneto IGT, but San Vincenzo has remained his benchmark everyday white and an Italian restaurant staple internationally.

milestones.event: First San Vincenzo Veneto IGT release from Roberto Anselmi
Anselmi resigns from Soave Consorzio; all wines become Veneto IGT

wines.description: Roberto Anselmi's San Vincenzo Veneto IGT: almond-blossom Garganega with 20% Chardonnay, the declassified Soave-equivalent that is one of the most recognisable Italian whites internationally.

wines.name: Roberto Anselmi Capitel Croce
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged 12 months in French oak barriques; aged on fine lees

taste.summary: Capitel Croce is Anselmi's barrel-aged 100% Garganega, named for a wayside cross on the Capitel Croce hill; oak-fermented with extended lees contact to produce a richer, more complex expression of the varietal with hazelnuts, golden apple and creamy texture.

pairings.why: The wine's hazelnut-cream weight and high acidity are perfect for rich crustacean and herb butter.
Risotto cooked in Soave with prawns is a regional dish ideally paired with the wine used in its preparation.

history.summary: Capitel Croce was Anselmi's early demonstration that Garganega could produce a world-class, age-worthy white wine when barrel-fermented; the wine helped establish the Monteforte hillside as a serious white wine site and influenced subsequent Soave producers.

milestones.event: First Capitel Croce barrel-fermented Garganega Veneto IGT release

wines.description: Roberto Anselmi's Capitel Croce Veneto IGT: barrel-fermented 100% Garganega from the Capitel Croce hill in Monteforte d'Alpone, with hazelnut-cream complexity and strong ageing potential.

wines.name: Roberto Anselmi I Capitelli
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged 18 months in French oak barriques

taste.summary: I Capitelli is Anselmi's late-harvest sweet Garganega, made from overripe and botrytised bunches from the Capitel Croce and Capitel Foscarino hills; rich, honeyed and apricot-dominated but lifted by the grape's natural acidity that keeps it from cloying.

pairings.why: The wine's almond-honey character echoes the marzipan richness of an Italian almond tart.
The sweet-salt contrast between wine and crystalline aged cheese is a classic Veneto tradition.
Simple fresh stone fruit amplifies the apricot register in this sweet Garganega without competing.

history.summary: I Capitelli was Anselmi's answer to the ancient Recioto di Soave tradition of sweet Garganega wine; made from late-harvested, botrytised grapes from the Capitel hills, it represents the sweet side of Garganega's complexity. The wine's name (the capitals) refers to the multiple Capitel vineyard plots it draws from.

milestones.event: First I Capitelli late-harvest Garganega Veneto IGT release

wines.description: Roberto Anselmi's I Capitelli Veneto IGT, sweet late-harvest 100% Garganega from botrytised Capitel hills bunches; honey-apricot richness balanced by high acidity.

wines.name: Le Salette Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Pergole Vece
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: Pergole Vece is from Le Salette's century-old pergola-trained Corvina vines at 450m elevation in Fumane, producing an Amarone of particular freshness and aromatic complexity; high altitude preserves acidity while old vines add concentration.

pairings.dish: Braised beef short rib with polenta
pairings.why: Firm tannins and concentration of dried fruit embrace the long-braised collagen richness.
The sharpest, most aged Veronese mountain cheese provides salt and fat contrast to the wine.

history.summary: Franco Scamperle established Le Salette in 1986 and identified the old pergola-trained Corvina vines on the high Fumane hillside as exceptional material. Pergole Vece (old pergolas) references these century-old training systems and is one of the most altitude-expressive Amarone in the Classico zone.

milestones.event: First Pergole Vece single-vineyard Amarone Classico release

wines.description: Le Salette's Pergole Vece Amarone Classico DOCG from century-old pergola-trained Corvina vines at 450m in Fumane; altitude freshness and old-vine concentration in a single-vineyard expression.

wines.name: Le Salette Valpolicella Classico Superiore
wines.oak_regime: Aged 12 months in large Slavonian oak; high-altitude vineyards in Fumane

taste.summary: Le Salette's Classico Superiore benefits from the high-altitude Fumane terroir that gives freshness and floral lift to the Corvina base; an elegant, food-friendly expression of the appellation.

pairings.dish: Risotto with Lugana radicchio and Taleggio
pairings.why: The wine's cherry fruit and high acidity balance the bitter radicchio and melting Taleggio.
Rosemary-scented pork pairs naturally with the wine's dried herb and cherry register.

history.summary: Le Salette's Valpolicella Classico Superiore is the estate's everyday expression from the high-altitude Fumane hillside vineyards, emphasising the freshness and floral character of high-elevation Corvina.

milestones.event: First Le Salette Valpolicella Classico Superiore release

wines.description: Le Salette's Valpolicella Classico Superiore DOC from high-altitude Fumane hillside Corvina vines; elegant, floral and food-friendly with Monica Scamperle's trademark freshness.

wines.name: Santa Sofia Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: Santa Sofia's Amarone is a traditional-style Classico from the historic Pedemonte estate, emphasising tobacco-leather complexity and long cellarability; the 16th-century villa setting adds heritage gravitas to a consistently well-made Amarone.

pairings.dish: Braised beef alla Veneziana with polenta
pairings.why: The wine's tobacco-leather richness is the textbook companion for Venetian-style braised beef.

pairings.dish: Prosciutto di San Daniele with melon
pairings.why: The sweet-salt of cured prosciutto and melon resonates with the wine's dried fruit concentration.

history.summary: Santa Sofia has operated from the historic Villa Santa Sofia in Pedemonte since 1811, but began bottling Amarone commercially in the 1940s under the Begnoni family. The Palladian-attributed loggia of the villa is a landmark in the Classico zone.

milestones.event: Santa Sofia estate established as a winery in historic Villa Santa Sofia
First Santa Sofia Amarone commercial bottling

wines.description: Santa Sofia's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG from the historic Pedemonte villa estate; traditional large-cask aged, tobacco-leather style from a historic producer.

verified.address_quoted: Via Ca' Dede 61, 37020 Pedemonte di San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Valentina Cubi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Morar
wines.oak_regime: Aged 2 years in large Slavonian oak and French tonneaux; certified organic production

taste.summary: Valentina Cubi's certified organic Amarone from the Fumane hillsides shows a distinctly earthy, mineral character from organic viticulture; the Oseleta addition contributes pepper spice and the high acidity reflects the altitude of the Fumane Classico zone.

pairings.why: The wine's peppery Oseleta and iron minerality match the gamey intensity of wild boar pasta.
Salty sheep's milk cheese accentuates the wine's dried-fruit and cedar complexity.

history.summary: Valentina Cubi, one of the few female winemakers in Valpolicella Classico, founded her estate in 1969 and converted to organic viticulture. Her Morar Amarone is made without chemical intervention from the Fumane hillside's old Corvina vines, producing a wine with an organic, earthy authenticity.

milestones.event: Valentina Cubi estate founded in Fumane
First Morar Amarone Classico release
Certified organic viticulture achieved

wines.description: Valentina Cubi's Morar Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG, certified organic from the Fumane hillside; earthy, mineral and peppery with old-vine Corvina and Oseleta intensity.

wines.name: Valentina Cubi Valpolicella Classico Superiore
wines.oak_regime: Aged 12 months in large Slavonian oak; organic viticulture

taste.summary: Valentina Cubi's organic Valpolicella Superiore shows the freshness of high-altitude Fumane fruit with earthy mineral character from organic farming; a genuinely characterful everyday Valpolicella with better-than-average expressiveness.

pairings.dish: Pasta e fagioli with smoked pancetta
pairings.why: The wine's tart cherry and earthy mineral character beautifully complement the smoky starchy soup.
Pressed roast chicken with herbs pairs well with the wine's dried herb and cherry register.

history.summary: Valentina Cubi's Valpolicella Classico Superiore is the estate's everyday organic offering from the Fumane hillside, made without additives or chemicals in the vineyard.

milestones.event: First Valentina Cubi Valpolicella Classico Superiore release

wines.description: Valentina Cubi's certified organic Valpolicella Classico Superiore DOC from Fumane; earthy, mineral cherry character with Oseleta pepper from a rare female-led Valpolicella estate.

wines.name: Nicolis Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: The Nicolis family's Amarone from the Villa Girardi estate in San Pietro in Cariano reflects a traditional, large-cask aged style with tobacco-leather character and solid structure; consistent quality across multiple vintages.

pairings.dish: Brasato al Amarone con polenta gialla
pairings.why: The classic Veronese pairing; Nicolis's tobacco-cedar notes reinforce the Amarone-braised beef dish.
Local Lessini mountain cheese complements the wine's dried-fruit complexity.

history.summary: The Nicolis family has farmed the Villa Girardi estate since 1951 and began Amarone production in the mid-1960s. Their traditional approach with large Slavonian casks produces a wine that reflects the appellation's classic tobacco-dried fruit character.

milestones.event: Nicolis family acquires Villa Girardi estate in San Pietro in Cariano
First Nicolis Amarone Classico commercial bottling

wines.description: Nicolis Angelo e Figli's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG from the Villa Girardi estate; traditional large-cask aged tobacco-leather style from a consistent family producer.

verified.address_quoted: Via Villa Girardi 29, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Nicolis Ambrosan Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 4 years in large Slavonian oak casks; selection from oldest estate vines

taste.summary: Ambrosan is the single-parcel selection from Nicolis's oldest vines on the Villa Girardi estate, given an extra year in large Slavonian casks compared to the estate Amarone; a more concentrated and austere expression of tobacco-cedar style with excellent ageing potential.

pairings.why: The iron mineral and cedar intensity of Ambrosan demands the collagen richness of oxtail braise.
The most intense Parmigiano provides sufficient salt and umami to match this concentrated wine.

history.summary: Ambrosan was developed as Nicolis's prestige selection from the oldest Villa Girardi vines, given longer wood ageing to produce a more austere, age-worthy Amarone for collectors.

milestones.event: First Ambrosan single-parcel Amarone Classico release from old Villa Girardi vines

wines.description: Nicolis's Ambrosan single-parcel Amarone Classico DOCG from oldest Villa Girardi vines; four years in large Slavonian oak producing a concentrated, austere tobacco-cedar style.

verified.address_quoted: Via Villa Girardi 29, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Viviani Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Campo Morar
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian oak casks and French tonneaux (500L)

taste.summary: Campo Morar (meadow of mulberries) is Viviani's single-vineyard Amarone from the highest plots on the Mazzano ridge in Negrar, at over 400 metres elevation; the altitude confers remarkable freshness and acidity to the dried-fruit concentration, producing a wine of particular precision and longevity.

pairings.dish: Venison rack with juniper berry sauce
pairings.why: The wine's iron-mineral precision and altitude freshness match the gamey intensity of venison.
The sharpest aged Veronese mountain cheese is the ideal regional match for Mazzano-altitude Amarone.

history.summary: Claudio Viviani established the Mazzano estate in 1993 and first released his Campo Morar Amarone from the highest ridge plots in 1997. The altitude freshness and precision of his Amarone have made it a connoisseur favourite among those who prefer a more structured, food-friendly style.

milestones.event: Viviani estate established in Mazzano di Negrar
First Campo Morar single-vineyard Amarone Classico release

wines.description: Viviani's Campo Morar single-vineyard Amarone Classico DOCG from the highest Mazzano ridge plots at 400m; altitude freshness and iron minerality distinguish this precisely structured small-production wine.

wines.name: Viviani Valpolicella Classico Superiore
wines.oak_regime: Aged 12 months in large Slavonian oak; high-altitude Mazzano terroir

taste.summary: Viviani's Classico Superiore from the high Mazzano ridge shows the altitude freshness that characterises all the estate's wines: iron mineral, tart cherry and mountain herbs in a lean, food-loving medium-red.

pairings.why: The wine's tart cherry acidity and herbal character cut through the fat of the regional salami.
Wild mushroom pasta and the wine's mineral-herb character are a classic mountain pairing.

history.summary: Viviani's estate Valpolicella Classico Superiore was established as the accessible tier from the Mazzano high-altitude plots, demonstrating that freshness and mineral precision extend to the lighter wine in the range.

milestones.event: First Viviani Valpolicella Classico Superiore release

wines.description: Viviani's Valpolicella Classico Superiore DOC from high-altitude Mazzano ridge; iron mineral and tart cherry character with mountain-herb freshness from Claudio Viviani's small estate.

wines.name: Cantina Valpolicella Negrar Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 2-3 years in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: The cooperative's estate Amarone Classico provides accessible quality from 230 family growers across the Classico zone; a solid, tobacco-cherry traditional Amarone at significantly better value than single-estate bottlings at comparable quality.

pairings.why: The wine's traditional tobacco-cherry profile is a reliable partner for Veronese-style stewed beef.
The cooperative's accessible price point and good structure make it a fine choice for truffle pizza.

history.summary: The Cantina Valpolicella Negrar cooperative has been producing Amarone since the 1960s, providing the 230 member grower families a route to market for their Classico zone fruit. The Domaso single-vineyard is the flagship, but the estate Amarone provides accessible quality at cooperative pricing.

milestones.event: Cantina Valpolicella Negrar cooperative founded
First cooperative Amarone Classico commercial release

wines.description: Cantina Valpolicella Negrar's cooperative Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG; a reliable traditional tobacco-cherry expression offering good value from 230 Classico zone grower families.

verified.address_quoted: Via Ca' Salgari 2, 37024 Negrar di Valpolicella (VR)

wines.name: Cantina Valpolicella Negrar Domaso Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian oak; fruit exclusively from the 14-hectare Maternigo hill parcel

taste.summary: Domaso is the cooperative's flagship single-vineyard Amarone from the 14-hectare Maternigo hill parcel, showing appreciably greater concentration and mineral precision than the estate-level wine; the earthy, tobacco-cedar style reflects the Maternigo terroir's calcareous character.

pairings.why: The signature Veronese pairing; Domaso's earthy mineral character anchors the starchy buckwheat polenta.
Sharp, crystalline aged Asiago from the Lessini hills is the quintessential local cheese pairing.
