history.summary: The cooperative identified the Maternigo hill parcel as capable of producing single-vineyard quality and established Domaso as their flagship bottling from this 14-hectare block. The name references the ancient Roman settlement that once occupied the Maternigo hilltop.

milestones.event: First Domaso single-vineyard Amarone Classico release from Maternigo parcel

wines.description: Cantina Valpolicella Negrar's Domaso single-vineyard Amarone Classico DOCG from the 14-hectare Maternigo hill; earthy mineral tobacco-cedar style with good structure at cooperative price.

verified.address_quoted: Via Ca' Salgari 2, 37024 Negrar di Valpolicella (VR)

wines.name: Zenato Valpolicella Superiore Ripassa
wines.oak_regime: Aged 18 months in Slavonian oak; refermented over dried Amarone skins

taste.summary: Zenato's Ripassa shows the Oseleta grape's peppery-spice influence even at the mid-tier level: good concentration from refermentation over dried skins with a distinguishing warmth from the native Oseleta component.

pairings.why: Spiced sausage in tomato sauce and the wine's pepper-cherry notes are a classic match.
The wine's high acidity and dried herb character complement rosemary-grilled lamb.

history.summary: Zenato's Ripassa was introduced as the estate's mid-tier offering, using the ripasso technique to add complexity and depth to their Oseleta-inflected Valpolicella blend.

milestones.event: First Zenato Valpolicella Ripassa DOC commercial release

wines.description: Zenato's Valpolicella Ripasso DOC with Oseleta pepper-spice; refermented over dried Amarone skins for added richness, aged 18 months in Slavonian oak.

verified.address_quoted: Via San Benedetto 8, 37019 Peschiera del Garda (VR)

wines.name: Brigaldara Il Vegro Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3-4 years in small French oak tonneaux (500L) and large Slavonian casks

taste.summary: Il Vegro is Brigaldara's prestige single-vineyard Amarone from the Il Vegro parcel; higher Oseleta concentration and extended ageing in a mix of small and large casks produce a richer, spicier expression than the estate Classico, with excellent concentration and structure.

pairings.dish: Roasted venison leg with chocolate sauce
pairings.why: The wine's dark chocolate notes and Oseleta pepper meet the game and cocoa richness of this preparation.
Sharp sheep's milk cheese brings salt and fat contrast to the wine's concentrated dark fruit.

history.summary: Il Vegro is Brigaldara's prestige statement from the Il Vegro parcel, featuring higher Oseleta and more complex oak ageing than the estate Classico. The name references the old Veronese dialect term for the elderberry, a wild plant common in the Valpolicella hillsides.

milestones.event: First Il Vegro single-vineyard Amarone Classico release from Brigaldara

wines.description: Brigaldara's Il Vegro single-vineyard prestige Amarone Classico DOCG; higher Oseleta component and extended mixed-oak ageing producing a richer, spicier expression of the estate's precise appassimento approach.

verified.address_quoted: Via Brigaldara 20, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Suavia Amedeo Recioto di Soave
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged 12 months in French oak barriques

taste.summary: Amedeo is Suavia's Recioto di Soave DOCG from the ancient Monte Carbonare vineyard; the sweet dried-Garganega wine balances luscious apricot-honey sweetness with the grape's natural high acidity, keeping the finish fresh and complex.

pairings.why: Stone fruit tart and almond echoes the wine's apricot-almond register perfectly.
The classic northern Italian cheese-and-honey plate is elevated by sweet Recioto di Soave.

history.summary: Amedeo is named for an ancestor of the Tessari family, and represents the estate's tribute to the ancient tradition of sweet Garganega wine that preceded both modern Soave and the dry table wine era.

milestones.event: First Amedeo Recioto di Soave DOCG release from Suavia

wines.description: Suavia's Amedeo Recioto di Soave DOCG, sweet dried-Garganega from Monte Carbonare centenarian vines; apricot-honey richness balanced by the grape's characteristic high acidity.

wines.name: Masi Serego Alighieri Vaio Armaron Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 4 years in large Slavonian oak casks; from Serego Alighieri estate vineyards

taste.summary: Vaio Armaron is the prestige Amarone from the historic Serego Alighieri estate, owned by descendants of Dante Alighieri, managed in partnership with Masi; the wine reflects the S. Ambrogio di Valpolicella hill terroir with a refined tobacco-cedar complexity and long cellaring potential.

pairings.why: The wine's tobacco-cedar sophistication and firm tannins make it an excellent match for slow-braised lamb.
The quintessential local pairing for Classico-zone Amarone; salt and mountain mineral character.

history.summary: The Serego Alighieri estate has been owned by descendants of the poet Dante Alighieri since the 14th century. Masi manages the estate in partnership with the family, producing Vaio Armaron (brave archer, an old Veronese phrase) from the S. Ambrogio di Valpolicella hillside vineyards as the estate's flagship Amarone.

milestones.event: Pietro Alighieri, son of Dante, acquires the Serego Alighieri estate
Masi begins managing Serego Alighieri wines in partnership
First Vaio Armaron Amarone Classico release under Masi management

wines.description: Masi's Serego Alighieri Vaio Armaron Amarone Classico DOCG, from the historic estate owned by Dante's descendants; tobacco-cedar complexity with four years in large Slavonian oak.

verified.address_quoted: Via Monteleone, 37015 Gargagnago di Sant'Ambrogio di Valpolicella (VR)

wines.name: Speri Valpolicella Classico Superiore La Roverina
wines.oak_regime: Aged 12 months in large Slavonian oak; partial ripasso technique

taste.summary: Speri's La Roverina Classico Superiore with partial ripasso technique; fresh cherry and tobacco character from the traditional large-cask approach, with added concentration from the ripasso step and the estate's characteristic acidity.

pairings.dish: Pasta con ragu di anatra (duck ragu pasta)
pairings.why: Duck ragu's richness is balanced by the wine's medium tannins and cherry acidity.
Sweet fig and silky prosciutto are a classic aperitivo pairing with Valpolicella-style reds.

history.summary: La Roverina was developed by Speri as the estate's accessible food-friendly tier, using the old Roverina name (from old Venetian dialect for the oak tree) to reference the large Slavonian oak ageing tradition.

milestones.event: First La Roverina Valpolicella Classico Superiore release

wines.description: Speri's La Roverina Valpolicella Classico Superiore DOC with partial ripasso; fresh cherry-tobacco, medium-body with Speri's characteristic high acidity.

verified.address_quoted: Via Fontana 14, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Speri Recioto della Valpolicella Classico Le Bine de Jago
wines.oak_regime: Aged 2 years in large Slavonian oak casks; fermentation arrested to retain residual sugar

taste.summary: Speri's Le Bine de Jago Recioto represents the traditional sweet red wine of Valpolicella before Amarone became dominant; the fermentation-arrested fruit preserves Corvina's characteristic high acidity beneath the luscious chocolate-cherry sweetness.

pairings.why: The wine's coffee-chocolate notes are a natural match for the Venetian-origin dessert.
Sweet Recioto bridges the creamy, mild blue saltiness of Dolcelatte beautifully.

history.summary: Speri's Le Bine de Jago ('the vines of Jago', using old Veronese dialect) is named for the Jago hillside in Fumane where the grapes for Recioto are carefully selected from the uppermost bunches with highest sugar concentration.

milestones.event: First Le Bine de Jago Recioto Classico release from the Jago hillside selection

wines.description: Speri's Le Bine de Jago Recioto della Valpolicella Classico DOCG, sweet passito red from the Jago hillside Fumane selection; traditional chocolate-cherry with high Corvina acidity.

verified.address_quoted: Via Fontana 14, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Pieropan Le Colombare Recioto di Soave
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged 12 months in French oak barriques; from the Le Colombare parcel

taste.summary: Pieropan's Recioto di Soave from the Le Colombare parcel is the most prestigious expression of the ancient sweet white wine tradition of the Soave zone; dried Garganega produces extraordinary apricot-honey concentration kept fresh by the varietal's trademark high acidity.

pairings.dish: Sbrisolona (crumble cake with almonds and polenta)
pairings.why: The almond-honey Recioto and crumbly Mantuan almond cake are a classic Northern Italian match.

pairings.dish: Aged Asiago with honey and walnuts
pairings.why: Honey-walnut cheese plate amplifies the wine's sweet-almond register and mineral acidity contrast.

history.summary: Recioto di Soave is the oldest wine style in the Soave zone, predating the dry Soave by centuries. Pieropan's Le Colombare (dovecote) is named for the pigeon loft on the Le Colombare parcel above Soave; Leonildo Pieropan revived the serious production of this style from selected upper-cluster dried Garganega.

milestones.event: Pieropan Le Colombare Recioto di Soave first released; revival of quality sweet Garganega tradition

wines.description: Pieropan's Le Colombare Recioto di Soave DOCG, dried-Garganega sweet white from the historic Le Colombare parcel; apricot-honey concentration with Garganega's characteristic high acidity.

wines.name: Santa Sofia Valpolicella Classico Superiore
wines.oak_regime: Aged 12 months in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: Santa Sofia's Classico Superiore is a reliable, food-friendly, accessible Valpolicella from the historic Pedemonte estate with cherry-tobacco character and good acidity.

pairings.why: The wine's cherry acidity and light tannin complement simple tomato-basil pasta perfectly.
Medium tannins and dried herb notes work well with simple herb-grilled poultry.

history.summary: Santa Sofia's Valpolicella Classico Superiore represents the estate's historic role as one of the Classico zone's accessible producers, offering easy-drinking Corvina-dominant reds from the Pedemonte terroir.

milestones.event: Santa Sofia Valpolicella Classico Superiore commercial production begins

wines.description: Santa Sofia's Valpolicella Classico Superiore DOC from the historic Pedemonte villa estate; accessible, cherry-tobacco Corvina-led red aged 12 months in large Slavonian oak.

verified.address_quoted: Via Ca' Dede 61, 37020 Pedemonte di San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Cantina di Soave Rocca Sveva Soave Classico
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel fermentation and ageing; no oak

taste.summary: The cooperative's entry-level Soave Classico DOC provides excellent access to the appellation's almond-blossom Garganega character at very accessible price; clean, fresh and food-versatile.

pairings.why: The wine's crisp acidity and light mineral freshness are a classic match for deep-fried mixed seafood.
The delicate white wine doesn't compete with the fresh dairy and tomato flavours.

history.summary: Cantina di Soave's entry-level Soave Classico DOC under the Rocca Sveva label provides volume access to the appellation from the cooperative's extensive network of 2,600 member growers across the Soave zone.

milestones.event: Rocca Sveva Soave Classico DOC entry-level label launched

wines.description: Cantina di Soave's Rocca Sveva Soave Classico DOC, the cooperative's fresh entry-level Garganega blend at an accessible price point from the Soave Classico zone.

wines.name: Bertani Amarone della Valpolicella Villa Novare
wines.oak_regime: Aged 6 years in large Slavonian oak casks; from the Villa Novare estate plots

taste.summary: Bertani's Villa Novare is the prestige Amarone from their historic Novare estate, given six years in Slavonian oak producing a wine of extraordinary complexity and longevity; perhaps the most authentically traditional Amarone Riserva-style bottling in the appellation.

pairings.dish: Stufato di manzo con tartufo nero
pairings.why: Braised beef with black truffle is the ultimate luxury pairing for the most aged traditional Amarone.
Sardinian aged pecorino's mineral sharpness is the ideal complement to very old-style Amarone.

history.summary: Bertani's Villa Novare Amarone is the prestige selection from the historic Novare cellars, given six years in large Slavonian casks rather than the standard eight or more. It represents Bertani's most concentrated single-estate expression and is released only in the best vintages.

milestones.event: First Villa Novare prestige Amarone release from Bertani

wines.description: Bertani's Villa Novare prestige Amarone della Valpolicella DOCG from the historic Novare estate; six-plus years in large Slavonian oak producing an extraordinarily traditional, long-lived wine.

wines.name: Tommasi Recioto della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 2 years in large Slavonian oak casks; fermentation arrested

taste.summary: Tommasi's Recioto Classico is made from the highest-selected Corvina and Corvinone bunches from the family's San Pietro in Cariano vineyards; chocolate-cherry and raisin sweetness balanced by traditional cask structure.

pairings.why: The wine's chocolate-cherry notes echo the molten dark chocolate of this dessert.
Sweet Recioto with pungent blue cheese and honey is a Veronese classic pairing.

history.summary: Tommasi's Recioto Classico maintains the ancient tradition of sweet red wine from the Valpolicella zone, using the family's highest-concentration Corvina-Corvinone selections from the Classico zone.

milestones.event: First Tommasi Recioto della Valpolicella Classico commercial release

wines.description: Tommasi's Recioto della Valpolicella Classico DOCG, sweet passito red from highest-selected Corvina-Corvinone bunches; chocolate-cherry with raisin sweetness from a consistent family estate.

verified.address_quoted: Via Ronchetto 2, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Inama Oratorio di San Lorenzo Soave Superiore DOCG
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel fermentation; aged 8 months on fine lees in stainless steel

taste.summary: Inama's Oratorio di San Lorenzo is the estate's second single-vineyard Soave Superiore from the adjacent San Lorenzo parcel, showing the same volcanic basalt minerality as Foscarino but with a slightly more herbal, fennel-accented character.

pairings.why: The wine's saline mineral character and herbal freshness balance the creamy salt cod purée.
Clean mineral Soave is the definitive accompaniment to grilled calamari with citrus.

history.summary: Inama's Oratorio di San Lorenzo (named for the historic chapel on the vineyard plot) was added as the estate's second single-vineyard Soave Superiore to demonstrate the distinct character of the San Lorenzo parcel versus the adjacent Foscarino.

milestones.event: First Oratorio di San Lorenzo Soave Superiore DOCG release from Inama

wines.description: Inama's Oratorio di San Lorenzo Soave Superiore DOCG, 100% Garganega from the historic San Lorenzo parcel on volcanic basalt; fennel-herbal mineral character with racy acidity.

wines.name: Tedeschi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico La Fabriseria
wines.oak_regime: Aged 3 years in large Slavonian oak casks; from the La Fabriseria single-vineyard parcel

taste.summary: La Fabriseria (old Venetian dialect for the forge) is Tedeschi's second single-vineyard Amarone from a plot on the Monte Olmi hillside; greater tobacco-iron concentration than the Capitel Monte Olmi, with a more austere, mineral-forward character.

pairings.dish: Braised duck leg with cinnamon and dried fruit
pairings.why: The wine's tobacco-iron complexity and dried-fruit concentration are ideal for spice-braised duck.
Crystalline Grana provides salt and umami contrast to the wine's intense dried-fruit density.

history.summary: La Fabriseria was added by Tedeschi alongside Capitel Monte Olmi to demonstrate the different character of adjacent plots on the Monte Olmi hillside; the La Fabriseria parcel produces a more austere, iron-mineral style compared to the richer Capitel Monte Olmi.

milestones.event: First La Fabriseria single-vineyard Amarone Classico release from Tedeschi

wines.description: Tedeschi's La Fabriseria single-vineyard Amarone Classico DOCG from the Monte Olmi hillside; austere, tobacco-iron style with good ageing potential from the six-generation estate.

verified.address_quoted: Via G. Verdi 4, 37020 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Dal Forno Romano Vigna Seré Passito Bianco
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged 2 years in French oak barriques

taste.summary: A rare white passito from Dal Forno, made from dried Moscato and Garganega grapes at the Illasi valley estate, applying the same concentration philosophy as the Amarone to a sweet white with orange blossom and apricot intensity.

pairings.why: The wine's orange blossom and apricot notes echo the pastry's citrus-ricotta character.
Ricotta lemon cake and the wine's apricot-honey acidity are a delicate match.

history.summary: Vigna Seré is Dal Forno's unusual white passito, made in tiny quantities from dried Moscato and Garganega at the Illasi estate; a curiosity piece from the producer known for ultra-concentrated Amarone, applying the same drying logic to white grapes.

milestones.event: First Dal Forno Vigna Seré Passito Bianco Veneto IGT release

wines.description: Dal Forno Romano's Vigna Seré Passito Bianco Veneto IGT, made from dried Moscato and Garganega at the Illasi estate; a rare white passito with orange blossom and apricot intensity.

wines.name: Allegrini Valpolicella Classico Superiore
wines.oak_regime: Aged 12 months in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: Allegrini's estate Classico Superiore reflects the house's high Corvinone proportion (45%) adding floral lift and softer tannin alongside Corvina's cherry-herb character; a bright, food-friendly everyday Valpolicella.

pairings.why: The quintessential Veronese pairing; cherry-herb Valpolicella and slow-simmered meat ragu.

pairings.dish: Grilled chicken with lemon and thyme
pairings.why: Light tannins and high acidity make this a perfect match for herb-grilled poultry.

history.summary: Allegrini's estate Classico Superiore has been produced since the family's early commercial winemaking days; the high Corvinone proportion reflects Giovanni Allegrini's belief in the variety's role in adding freshness and floral character to the Valpolicella blend.

milestones.event: Allegrini Valpolicella Classico Superiore commercial production established

wines.description: Allegrini's Valpolicella Classico Superiore DOC with 45% Corvinone for floral lift alongside Corvina; a bright, food-friendly cherry-herb red for everyday drinking.

wines.name: Gini Soave Classico
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel fermentation; aged 5 months on fine lees

taste.summary: Gini's estate Soave Classico 100% Garganega captures the family's 17th-century vine heritage in a fresh, mineral, everyday expression; the racy acidity and almond-blossom purity of the volcanic Monteforte basalt terroir at an accessible price.

pairings.why: The wine's racy acidity and mineral freshness are ideal for fried crab and herb aioli.
Light, mineral, acidic Soave is the natural match for Venetian chilled seafood salad.

history.summary: Gini's estate Soave Classico is the entry-level expression from the family's Monteforte d'Alpone basalt vineyards, available at accessible pricing and representing the consistent quality of one of Soave's most respected estates.

milestones.event: First Gini estate Soave Classico DOC commercial release

wines.description: Gini's estate Soave Classico DOC, 100% Garganega from the family's volcanic basalt Monteforte vineyards; racy acidity, almond-blossom freshness at an accessible price.

wines.name: Lorenzo Begali Valpolicella Classico Superiore
wines.oak_regime: Aged 12 months in large Slavonian oak; old-vine Ca' Bianca fruit

taste.summary: Begali's Valpolicella Classico Superiore from Ca' Bianca old vines shows the same mineral-iron freshness as the estate's Amarone in a lighter register; a food-friendly expression of traditional San Pietro in Cariano Corvina.

pairings.dish: Pasta con sarde in saor (Venetian sweet-sour sardines with pasta)
pairings.why: The wine's high acidity and iron mineral character balance the vinegar-raisin sweet-sour of sarde in saor.
Baked ham's mild richness and the wine's light tannin make for an easy regional pairing.

history.summary: Lorenzo Begali's Valpolicella Classico Superiore is made from the same old Ca' Bianca Corvina vines as the Amarone Monte Ca' Bianca; while lighter in style, it retains the estate's characteristic iron-mineral freshness.

milestones.event: First Lorenzo Begali Valpolicella Classico Superiore release

wines.description: Lorenzo Begali's Valpolicella Classico Superiore DOC from old Ca' Bianca Corvina vines; iron mineral freshness and tart cherry in a lighter register from this respected traditional estate.

verified.address_quoted: Via Cengia 10, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Zenato Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Riserva Sergio Zenato
wines.oak_regime: Aged 5 years in a combination of large Slavonian oak and new French barriques; Riserva selection

taste.summary: The Riserva Sergio Zenato is the estate's memorial bottling to its founder, made only in exceptional vintages and aged five years in oak; the highest Oseleta proportion and extended ageing produce Zenato's most structured and concentrated wine, with extraordinary longevity.

pairings.dish: Beef Wellington with black truffle sauce
pairings.why: The concentration and complexity of this riserva-level Amarone demands a truly luxurious preparation.
The sharpest local aged cheese provides sufficient salinity to balance the wine's immense concentration.

history.summary: Named in memory of Sergio Zenato, who founded the estate in 1960 and pioneered Oseleta in the Classico blends, the Riserva is made only in best vintages from the oldest estate parcels with maximum drying and five years of oak ageing.

milestones.event: First Riserva Sergio Zenato Amarone Classico release in memorial to the founder

wines.description: Zenato's Riserva Sergio Zenato Amarone Classico DOCG, made only in the best vintages with five years oak ageing; the memorial wine to the estate's founder and the most concentrated expression of the Oseleta-infused house style.

verified.address_quoted: Via San Benedetto 8, 37019 Peschiera del Garda (VR)

wines.name: Masi Toar Rosso Veronese IGT
wines.oak_regime: Aged 2 years in a combination of large Slavonian oak and small French barriques; partial drying of Oseleta

taste.summary: Masi's Toar is a 30% Oseleta-dominant Veronese IGT that deliberately showcases the rarely-used indigenous variety; partial drying of the Oseleta component adds concentration and the grape's characteristic dark spice and iron mineral character.

pairings.why: Peppery Oseleta spice and iron mineral are the natural partner for gamey wild boar sausage.

pairings.dish: Aged Asiago d'Allevo with black pepper honey
pairings.why: The cheese plate's pepper and age contrasts elegantly with the wine's Oseleta-iron register.

history.summary: Toar (an old Veronese word for a rocky volcanic cliff face) was created by Masi specifically to rehabilitate Oseleta, the indigenous Valpolicella variety that had almost disappeared; by making it 30% of the blend, Masi demonstrated its distinctive spice-iron contribution and helped spark the modern revival of the variety.

milestones.event: First Masi Toar Veronese IGT release, pioneering Oseleta's commercial revival

wines.description: Masi's Toar Rosso Veronese IGT: 70% Corvina with 30% partially-dried Oseleta, pioneering the indigenous variety's commercial revival with peppery iron-mineral intensity.

verified.address_quoted: Via Monteleone, 37015 Gargagnago di Sant'Ambrogio di Valpolicella (VR)

wines.name: Nicolis Valpolicella Classico Superiore
wines.oak_regime: Aged 12 months in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: The Nicolis estate Classico Superiore offers an honest, traditional Valpolicella from the Villa Girardi hillside; cherry-tobacco character with the freshness typical of a classic-zone Corvina aged gently in large casks.

pairings.why: High acidity and medium tannins are the quintessential match for Northern Italian meat ragu.

pairings.dish: Baked chicken with thyme and garlic
pairings.why: Light herb-driven preparation matches the wine's tobacco-herb character perfectly.

history.summary: Nicolis has been producing Valpolicella Classico Superiore from the Villa Girardi estate since the 1970s alongside Amarone, offering an accessible tier that demonstrates the freshness of the family's Classico-zone fruit.

milestones.event: First Nicolis Valpolicella Classico Superiore commercial release

wines.description: Nicolis's Valpolicella Classico Superiore DOC from Villa Girardi in San Pietro in Cariano; traditional large-cask aged cherry-tobacco Corvina at an accessible price.

verified.address_quoted: Via Villa Girardi 29, 37029 San Pietro in Cariano (VR)

wines.name: Ca' La Bionda Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Castel Caina
wines.oak_regime: Aged 4 years in large Slavonian oak; single-vineyard selection from Castel Caina parcel

taste.summary: Castel Caina is Ca' La Bionda's premium single-vineyard selection from the highest Marano valley plots at the foot of the ancient Caina hill; the volcanic soils produce even greater mineral depth and acidity than the estate Classico, with outstanding ageing potential.

pairings.dish: Lamb shoulder slow-roasted with mountain herbs
pairings.why: The wine's iron mineral character and high acidity balance the fat and herbaceous complexity of mountain lamb.
The sharpest aged Veronese mountain cheese amplifies the wine's mineral depth.

history.summary: The Castellani family identified the Castel Caina parcels as their highest-quality fruit and established the single-vineyard Amarone to showcase the volcanic-soil mineral intensity that distinguishes the upper Marano valley from lower-altitude Classico producers.

milestones.event: First Castel Caina single-vineyard Amarone Classico release from Ca' La Bionda

wines.description: Ca' La Bionda's Castel Caina single-vineyard Amarone Classico DOCG from the highest Marano valley volcanic plots; exceptional mineral depth and acidity in a traditional large-cask expression.

wines.name: Le Salette Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Classico
wines.oak_regime: Aged 2 years in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: Le Salette's estate Amarone Classico, from the high-altitude Fumane vineyards farmed by Monica Scamperle, shows the distinctive freshness and orange-peel note that characterise the estate's high-elevation fruit; a more approachable entry to the Le Salette style than the Pergole Vece.

pairings.dish: Braised pork cheeks with root vegetables
pairings.why: Medium tannin and cherry-tobacco notes complement the fatty richness of long-braised pork.
Northern Italian hard cheese with salt and umami is a reliable complement to classic-zone Amarone.

history.summary: Le Salette's estate Amarone Classico was established by Franco Scamperle shortly after founding the estate in 1986, drawing from the high Fumane hillside fruit that would later produce the single-vineyard Pergole Vece.

milestones.event: First Le Salette Amarone Classico estate release

wines.description: Le Salette's estate Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG from high-altitude Fumane fruit; the accessible entry to Monica Scamperle's fresh, orange-peel inflected Amarone style.

wines.name: Nino Franco Rustico Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel Charmat second fermentation

taste.summary: The benchmark entry-level Prosecco Superiore DOCG from Nino Franco. Pale straw with fine persistent perlage; white stone fruit and floral notes lead to a crisp, dry finish.

pairings.why: Effervescence and high acidity cut through cured meats and marinated vegetables.
Delicate floral character complements lean lake fish without overpowering.
Lactic softness of the cheese echoes the wine's light cream texture.

history.summary: Rustico has been the entry-point to the Nino Franco portfolio since the house was founded in 1919 in Valdobbiadene. Primo Franco relaunched the label in the 1970s as a quality benchmark for the zone, focusing on pure Glera from estate and contracted Conegliano Valdobbiadene hillside plots.

milestones.event: Nino Franco founds the Valdobbiadene house and begins producing Rustico as the core expression.
Primo Franco assumes winemaking and sharpens the Rustico style to emphasise freshness over sweetness.

wines.description: The Nino Franco house classic since 1919. Rustico is a dry, lively Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG with white-peach aromatics and racy acidity, the definitive aperitivo Prosecco.

wines.name: Nino Franco Grave di Stecca Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel Charmat

taste.summary: A single-hill Cartizze grand cru expression from the Stecca parcel, with richer fruit, honeyed florality and greater complexity than the standard Rustico, yet retaining the hallmark lightness of the hill.

pairings.why: Off-dry profile and floral notes harmonise with fruit-based pastry without clashing.
Sweetness of the wine amplifies the natural sweetness of seared shellfish.
Rich olive oil texture of the cod is lifted by the wine's effervescence and fruit.

history.summary: The Cartizze hill is a 107-hectare grand cru within the Valdobbiadene DOCG. Nino Franco has held plots on the Stecca parcel since the 1980s. Grave di Stecca, meaning 'stony ground of Stecca', references the rocky south-facing terraces. It is one of the house's most prestigious single-site expressions.

milestones.event: Nino Franco first releases Grave di Stecca as a single-vineyard Cartizze expression.

wines.description: Cartizze grand-cru Prosecco from Nino Franco's Stecca parcel on the storied 107-hectare hill. Honeyed florality, ripe nectarine and fine acidity; the off-dry style showcases Glera at its most expressive.

wines.name: Nino Franco Primo Franco Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore
wines.oak_regime: No oak; temperature-controlled Charmat method

taste.summary: The vintage-dated prestige cuvee of Nino Franco named for founder Primo Franco. Brut in style, with greater texture and a longer Charmat contact than Rustico; mineral and elegant.

pairings.why: The wine's stone-fruit lift contrasts with the bitter-sweet character of Treviso radicchio.
Briny shellfish is refreshed by the wine's citrus and mineral thread.
Light body and high acidity cleanse the palate after creamy ricotta or mozzarella.

history.summary: Created as a tribute to Primo Franco, who transformed Nino Franco from a local producer to an internationally recognized quality house. Primo Franco is a vintage-dated single-harvest expression that preserves the unique character of each year's harvest from selected Valdobbiadene hillsides.

milestones.event: Primo Franco vintage cuvee introduced as the top-tier Valdobbiadene DOCG bottling.

wines.description: The prestige vintage cuvee from Nino Franco, named after founder Primo Franco. Brut Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG with mineral complexity and fine persistent perlage.

wines.name: Nino Franco Rive di Pergola Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore
wines.classification: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Rive di Pergola
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel Charmat

taste.summary: The Rive designation identifies a single-municipality harvest on steep hillsides. Di Pergola offers stony minerality and tighter acidity than the multi-commune Rustico, reflecting the specific clay-limestone terroir.

pairings.why: Mineral and saline character echoes the brininess of clams.
Light effervescence and crispness match the diverse flavours of Venetian nibbles.
Racy acidity and mineral thread complement the delicate flesh of Garda perch.

history.summary: The Rive category was established by the Conegliano Valdobbiadene Consorzio in 2009 to recognise single-municipality terroir. Nino Franco's Rive di Pergola is harvested from the municipality of Pergola on steep slopes, declassifying blended production to emphasise site expression.

milestones.event: Consorzio launches the Rive category for single-commune Prosecco Superiore DOCG.
Nino Franco releases the first vintage of Rive di Pergola.

wines.description: Single-municipality Rive expression from Nino Franco. Pergola's steep clay-limestone slopes give this brut Prosecco Superiore DOCG a pronounced mineral and citrus character.

wines.name: Bisol Cartizze Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method in temperature-controlled tanks

taste.summary: Rich and aromatic expression from the 107-ha Cartizze grand cru hill. Bisol's long tenure on the hill and multi-generational vine stock give this off-dry sparkler a layered complexity unusual in Prosecco.

pairings.why: The off-dry, honey-toned Cartizze is the traditional Venetian match for festive pastry.
Stone fruit notes in the wine mirror the fruit in the tart for a seamless pairing.
Creamy texture of the wine cushions the rich set cream of panna cotta.

history.summary: The Bisol family has owned vines on the Cartizze hill since at least the 19th century. The Cartizze designation is the apex of the Valdobbiadene DOCG hierarchy: 107 ha of single hill shared by approximately 140 growers. Bisol is consistently among the top references for the designation.

milestones.event: Bisol family documented owning Cartizze parcels in estate records.
Cartizze granted its own recognition within the Valdobbiadene DOC.

wines.description: Bisol's flagship Cartizze grand cru expression. Off-dry with ripe peach, lychee and acacia honey from the 107-ha Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze DOCG hill.

wines.name: Bisol Crede Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut
wines.oak_regime: No oak; extended Charmat method

taste.summary: The flagship multi-varietal Valdobbiadene Brut from Bisol. 'Crede' refers to the clay-limestone (crete) soils of the Valdobbiadene hills. Inclusion of indigenous Verdiso, Perera and Bianchetta adds aromatic complexity absent in single-varietal Prosecco.

pairings.why: Crisp acidity and fine perlage cut through fried seafood perfectly.
Salt and fat of anchovy are balanced by the wine's fruit and effervescence.
Mineral character complements the earthy bitterness of artichoke.

history.summary: Crede was conceived as Bisol's statement cuvee for the Valdobbiadene DOCG, incorporating the estate's indigenous varieties beyond Glera. The name references the local term for the clay and limestone soils that define the sub-zone's mineral character.

milestones.event: Bisol launches Crede as a multi-varietal brut blending Glera with Verdiso, Perera and Bianchetta.

wines.description: The flagship multi-varietal Brut from Bisol 1542. Crede blends Glera with indigenous Verdiso, Perera and Bianchetta Trevigiana from clay-limestone hillside plots for a complex, mineral Prosecco Superiore DOCG.

wines.name: Bisol Relio Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: Extra Dry in Prosecco parlance is slightly sweeter (12-17 g/l residual sugar) than Brut. Relio is a soft, fruit-forward style with rounded acidity, suited to lighter food pairings and aperitivo moments.

pairings.why: Light sweetness of the wine complements the delicate salt-and-fat balance of the cured ham.
Classic Prosecco Extra Dry and strawberry pairing; the sweetness levels align.
Fruit-forward softness pairs well with citrus-dressed green salads.

history.summary: Relio was introduced to address consumer demand for the historically popular Extra Dry category, which remains the best-selling Prosecco style. Bisol's version uses pure Glera from hillside plots for a clean, approachable expression of the appellation.

milestones.event: Bisol releases Relio as a dedicated Extra Dry Valdobbiadene DOCG label.

wines.description: Bisol's Extra Dry Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG. Ripe pear and elderflower aromatics, soft off-dry palate; the most approachable expression in the Bisol range.

wines.name: Bisol Rive di Campea Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore
wines.classification: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Rive di Campea
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel Charmat

taste.summary: The Rive di Campea sits at higher elevation than most Valdobbiadene Rive sites. The altitude emphasises acidity and mineral character, producing one of the zone's most vertically inclined expressions.

pairings.why: Flinty minerality and racy acidity are the classic match for raw oysters.
The citrus and chalk thread lifts delicate raw fish without masking it.
Herb and citrus notes align with grilled vegetables dressed in lemon.

history.summary: Rive di Campea is one of Bisol's two Rive single-municipality expressions, alongside Rive di Guia. Campea is one of the higher-altitude municipalities of the Valdobbiadene DOCG zone; the 2011 release followed the Consorzio's introduction of the Rive category in 2009.

milestones.event: Rive category launched by the Conegliano Valdobbiadene DOCG Consorzio.
Bisol releases Rive di Campea to highlight elevation-driven minerality.

wines.description: High-altitude Rive expression from Bisol 1542. Campea's elevation amplifies acidity and chalk minerality, producing a flinty, racy Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG.

wines.name: Bortolomiol Senior Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry
wines.oak_regime: No oak; classic Charmat method

taste.summary: The flagship Extra Dry from Bortolomiol, founded in 1949 by Giuliano Bortolomiol. Named 'Senior' in tribute to the founder, this is a classic off-dry Valdobbiadene DOCG with the balance of sweetness and freshness that made the Extra Dry category popular.

pairings.why: Light sweetness of Extra Dry contrasts the saltiness of bresaola.
Soft acidity does not overwhelm delicate raw tuna.
Off-dry palate and coffee notes in tiramisu create a gentle contrast.

history.summary: Giuliano Bortolomiol founded the Valdobbiadene house in 1949 and pioneered consistent quality-oriented Prosecco Superiore production at a time when the zone was considered ordinary. His daughter Giuliana now manages the estate. Senior is the original label dating to the founding year.

milestones.event: Giuliano Bortolomiol founds the Valdobbiadene house and launches the first Senior bottling.
Giuliana Bortolomiol takes over management of the family estate.

wines.description: The founding label of Bortolomiol, first produced in 1949. Senior is a softly off-dry Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG with white peach and gentle acidity.

wines.name: Bortolomiol Ius Naturae Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut Nature
wines.oak_regime: No oak; extended Charmat with zero dosage

taste.summary: Brut Nature (zero dosage, under 3 g/l residual sugar) is the driest legal Prosecco style. Ius Naturae showcases the natural grape sugar without added liqueur, emphasising Glera's inherent acidity and mineral character.

pairings.why: Zero-dosage dryness and saline thread is the ideal backdrop for briny oysters.
Clean and racy finish cleanses the palate between delicate slices of raw fish.
The wine's citrus and chalk echoes a lemon-dressed fresh cheese.

history.summary: The Brut Nature style emerged in Prosecco in the 2010s as producers responded to demand for drier, lower-intervention expressions. Bortolomiol's Ius Naturae (Latin: 'natural law') was one of the first dedicated zero-dosage Valdobbiadene DOCG releases, reflecting Giuliana Bortolomiol's commitment to minimal intervention.

milestones.event: Bortolomiol releases Ius Naturae as a zero-dosage Brut Nature expression of Valdobbiadene DOCG.

wines.description: Bortolomiol's zero-dosage Brut Nature Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG. Ius Naturae is one of the driest expressions in the zone, with racy acidity and pronounced mineral character.

wines.name: Bortolomiol Bandarossa Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: Bandarossa (red ribbon) is a fruitier, more aromatic Extra Dry than Senior. Designed for pairing with fruit-forward desserts and aromatic preparations, it expresses the warmer side of Valdobbiadene's Glera.

pairings.why: The wine's stone-fruit and almond notes echo the classic Venetian tart.
Off-dry softness tames mild spice while the floral aromatics complement Asian herbs.
Creamy mascarpone is complemented by the wine's peachy sweetness.

history.summary: Bandarossa was introduced in the 1980s as a more aromatic, fruit-expressive counterpart to Senior. The red ribbon branding distinguished it visually in the Italian market and it became one of Bortolomiol's steady volume labels.

milestones.event: Bandarossa introduced as a distinctly aromatic Extra Dry alongside Senior.

wines.description: Bortolomiol's aromatic Extra Dry Valdobbiadene DOCG. Bandarossa (red ribbon) leans into ripe peach, wisteria and soft sweetness for a fruit-forward aperitivo style.

wines.name: Bortolomiol Filanda Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut
wines.oak_regime: No oak; longer autolysis in Charmat

taste.summary: Filanda (silk mill) is the top non-Cartizze label from Bortolomiol, employing a longer extended Charmat contact for added yeast-derived complexity. A brut style with more depth than the entry-level range.

pairings.why: The bread and cream notes in the wine complement the earthiness of porcini risotto.
High acidity and fine perlage cut through light batter and rich prawn flesh.
Complexity of the Filanda matches the richness of burrata and truffle.

history.summary: Filanda was launched to represent the premium tier of Bortolomiol's Valdobbiadene DOCG production. The name references the historic silk mills (filatoio) that were the economic backbone of the Treviso hills before viticulture became dominant. Extended Charmat contact was a deliberate stylistic choice to add complexity.

milestones.event: Bortolomiol launches Filanda as a premium Brut using extended autolysis in Charmat.

wines.description: Bortolomiol's premium Brut Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG. Filanda uses extended Charmat autolysis for yeast-derived complexity above the standard range.

wines.name: Ruggeri Giustino B Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: The historical benchmark of Ruggeri, first produced when the house was founded in 1950. Giustino B is named after Giustino Bisol, who co-founded the house; this Extra Dry expression became one of the most recognised Prosecco Superiore labels in the zone.

pairings.why: The off-dry profile with melon fruit is a natural match for the classic Italian starter.
Light sweetness tempers the smoke and salt of the salmon.
Soft effervescence and gentle sweetness cut the richness of melted cheese.

history.summary: Ruggeri was co-founded by Giustino Bisol in 1950, and the house's flagship Extra Dry carries his name. The Giustino B label has been one of the consistently acclaimed expressions of the Extra Dry category and helped define the accessible-but-quality tier of Valdobbiadene DOCG production.

milestones.event: Ruggeri founded in Valdobbiadene; Giustino B Extra Dry launched as the house's flagship.

wines.description: The house flagship from Ruggeri since its 1950 founding. Giustino B is a classic Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry with consistent white-peach fruit and soft off-dry finish.

wines.name: Ruggeri Oro Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: Ruggeri Oro is the accessible volume entry-level Extra Dry, designed for everyday aperitivo occasions. Consistent, clean and fruit-forward with the classic Prosecco Superiore profile.

pairings.why: Light and clean sparkling works as a universal aperitivo wine.
Non-challenging fruit and gentle sweetness suits casual party food.

history.summary: Oro (gold) is Ruggeri's original accessible label, pre-dating Giustino B as the broad market line. Positioned below Giustino B in quality and price, it provides volume consistency and is widely distributed across the Italian on-trade.

milestones.event: Ruggeri launches Oro as the accessible everyday label alongside the flagship Giustino B.

wines.description: Ruggeri's entry-level Extra Dry Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG. Oro is clean, fruit-forward and accessible; a reliable everyday aperitivo Prosecco.

wines.name: Ruggeri Cartizze Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: Ruggeri's Cartizze expression from the storied 107-hectare grand cru hill. Off-dry and aromatic with richer fruit and honey than the standard Valdobbiadene range, showing the concentration that the south-facing Cartizze slopes produce.

pairings.why: The classic Venetian festive pairing: Cartizze softness and sweetness match the enriched dough.
Tropical fruit in the wine echoes the mango and the cream cushions its sweetness.

history.summary: Ruggeri acquired Cartizze plots in the early decades after the hill's official recognition. The Cartizze designation was codified within the Valdobbiadene DOC in 1969 and elevated within the DOCG. Ruggeri's expression is noted for balance and complexity.

milestones.event: Cartizze granted its own sub-denomination within the Valdobbiadene DOC.
Ruggeri first releases its Cartizze label.

wines.description: Ruggeri's grand cru Cartizze expression. Off-dry and aromatic with ripe apricot, lychee and honey from the 107-ha Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze DOCG hill.

wines.name: Ruggeri Quartese Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: Ruggeri's brut style for occasions calling for a leaner, drier Prosecco Superiore than the classic Extra Dry. Quartese is named after a Valdobbiadene locality and emphasises the zone's mineral freshness.

pairings.why: Dry freshness and mineral thread are ideal with grilled sea bass.
Brut acidity cleanses between bites of creamy dressing.
Versatile brut works across the diversity of Venetian bar snacks.

history.summary: Quartese was introduced as Ruggeri's answer to growing demand for dry Brut Prosecco, distinct from the Extra Dry heritage of the house. The name references the Quartese locality of the Valdobbiadene hills.

milestones.event: Ruggeri launches Quartese Brut as a dry-style complement to the Extra Dry flagship.

wines.description: Ruggeri's dry Brut Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG. Quartese emphasises citrus mineral freshness from Valdobbiadene hillside Glera.

wines.name: Adami Vigneto Giardino Rive di Colbertaldo Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut
wines.classification: Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Rive di Colbertaldo
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method with controlled temperature

taste.summary: One of the most admired single-vineyard Rive expressions in the Conegliano Valdobbiadene DOCG. The Giardino parcel sits on the steep Colbertaldo hillside; steep gradient, poor soils and attentive pruning give the wine its notable concentration and precision.

pairings.why: Precision acidity and stone-fruit lift complement the sweetness of lobster.
The wine's citrus freshness cuts through the tuna-cream dressing on veal.
Mineral acidity contrasts with rich hollandaise while echoing the green vegetable.

history.summary: The Vigneto Giardino (garden vineyard) has been in the Adami family for generations. When the Consorzio launched the Rive category in 2009 to identify single-municipality harvests, Adami was among the first producers to release a Rive bottling. Rive di Colbertaldo is now among the most sought-after of the forty-plus individual Rive denominations.

milestones.event: Rive category launched; Adami's Vigneto Giardino on Colbertaldo hillside included from the first vintage.

wines.description: Among the most acclaimed Rive single-vineyard expressions in the Valdobbiadene DOCG. The Giardino parcel on the steep Colbertaldo hillside produces a Brut Prosecco Superiore with stone-fruit precision and mineral depth.

wines.name: Adami dei Casel Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: The entry-level Extra Dry from Adami. Dei Casel (meaning 'of the hamlet') has been the house's everyday accessible label since the early decades of production. Consistent, clean and approachable.

pairings.why: Light effervescence and sweetness cut through the fried dough without masking it.
Fresh tomato and mozzarella are lifted by the wine's delicate fruit.

history.summary: Adami was founded in 1920 at Vidor in the Rive di Colbertaldo zone. The Dei Casel label has been a constant of the house portfolio, representing the accessible entry point to Adami's Prosecco Superiore range.

milestones.event: Adami founded at Vidor; Dei Casel becomes the house entry-point label.

wines.description: Adami's accessible Extra Dry Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG. Dei Casel is a consistent everyday label with ripe peach fruit and gentle off-dry finish.

wines.name: Adami Bosco di Gica Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: Bosco di Gica (Gica's wood) is a mid-range Brut from Adami, positioned between Dei Casel and the Rive Giardino. The Brut style emphasises the Valdobbiadene zone's fresh, mineral character without the Ex Dry sweetness.

pairings.why: Brut acidity and fine perlage are the classic match for light fried seafood.
The wine's citrus and mineral thread aligns with lemon-dressed salads.
Dry, mineral character cuts through smoky richness.

history.summary: Bosco di Gica was introduced to bridge Adami's entry-level and single-vineyard tiers. The name derives from a wooded parcel on the estate. It represents the Adami house style in Brut format: clean, precise and food-friendly.

milestones.event: Adami launches Bosco di Gica Brut as a mid-range alternative to Dei Casel Extra Dry.

wines.description: Adami's mid-range Brut Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG. Bosco di Gica bridges the entry-level Extra Dry and the prestige Rive Giardino with clean mineral apple and citrus character.

wines.name: Villa Sandi Il Fresco Prosecco DOC Treviso Brut
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: Villa Sandi's volume Prosecco DOC expression. Il Fresco is the globally distributed everyday label from the Palladian villa estate in Crocetta del Montello; consistent, approachable and affordable at the DOC level.

pairings.why: An accessible everyday Prosecco DOC suited to informal aperitivo settings.
Fresh tomato acidity and the wine's clean fruit are naturally compatible.

history.summary: Villa Sandi was founded in 1622 as a Palladian villa in the Marca Trevigiana. The Moretti Polegato family developed the Prosecco production wing from the late 20th century. Il Fresco became the volume entry label distributed globally to introduce consumers to the Prosecco DOC category.

milestones.event: Villa Sandi launches Il Fresco as the international-market accessible Prosecco DOC.

wines.description: Villa Sandi's international-market Prosecco DOC Treviso Brut. Il Fresco is a clean, accessible everyday sparkling from the historic Palladian villa estate.

wines.name: Villa Sandi Cartizze Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze Dry
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: Villa Sandi's Cartizze Dry is at the sweeter end of the Cartizze spectrum (17-32 g/l residual sugar). The grand cru hill's concentration of sunny exposure and south-facing slopes amplifies fruit ripeness into ripe peach and mango territory.

pairings.why: Sweetness of the wine matches stone-fruit pastry without excess.
Cream and honey notes in the wine complement cream-filled pastry.
Dried fruit sweetness echoes the wine's tropical notes.

history.summary: Villa Sandi has owned Cartizze vineyards since acquiring plots on the grand cru hill in the 1970s and 1980s. The Dry style (sweeter than Extra Dry and Brut) was a traditional preference for Cartizze, associated with the richer fruit concentration the hill naturally produces.

milestones.event: Villa Sandi first releases a dedicated Cartizze label from estate vineyards on the hill.

wines.description: Villa Sandi's Cartizze Dry expression from the 107-ha DOCG grand cru hill. Ripe peach, mango and honey in a soft medium-sweet style; the sweeter end of the Cartizze spectrum.

wines.name: Villa Sandi Opere Trevigiane Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut
wines.oak_regime: No oak; extended Charmat

taste.summary: Opere Trevigiane is Villa Sandi's premium DOCG Brut, positioned between Il Fresco DOC and the Cartizze grand cru. It uses Glera from selected hillside parcels within the Valdobbiadene DOCG zone for a more structured brut expression.

pairings.why: Mineral and high-acid brut amplifies the briny clam and garlic character.
Clean citrus thread in the wine mirrors the lemon in the sauce.
Crisp effervescence refreshes between bites of substantial fried food.

history.summary: Opere Trevigiane (works of the Trevisian region) was launched to represent Villa Sandi's DOCG quality tier more precisely than the broader DOC Treviso Il Fresco. The label draws from hillside plots in the Valdobbiadene DOCG zone, giving a step up in complexity.

milestones.event: Villa Sandi introduces Opere Trevigiane as the premium Valdobbiadene DOCG Brut above Il Fresco.

wines.description: Villa Sandi's premium DOCG Brut tier. Opere Trevigiane draws from hillside Valdobbiadene DOCG parcels for mineral acidity and precise stone-fruit character above the DOC entry-level.

wines.name: Col Vetoraz Valdobbiadene Superiore di Cartizze
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: Col Vetoraz is uniquely positioned above Santo Stefano at the edge of the Cartizze hill, giving its Cartizze expression exceptional altitude and south-facing exposure. The result is an opulent, aromatic Cartizze with great aromatic intensity and length.

pairings.why: Creamy custard and the wine's honey-cream texture are mutually enhancing.
Floral and off-dry character harmonises with vanilla-scented shellfish.
Stone-fruit in both the wine and gelato create a seamless pairing.

history.summary: Col Vetoraz was founded in 1993 specifically to produce Cartizze and premium Prosecco Superiore from the hillsides above Santo Stefano. The winery sits at higher elevation than most Valdobbiadene estates, exploiting the cooler microclimate for aromatic complexity.

milestones.event: Col Vetoraz founded by three families above Santo Stefano, with Cartizze as the core focus.

wines.description: Col Vetoraz's benchmark Cartizze DOCG expression from hillside vineyards above Santo Stefano. Opulent apricot, honey and tropical fruit with notable complexity from the estate's elevated position.

verified.address_quoted: Loc. Col Vetoraz 1, 31040 Santo Stefano di Valdobbiadene (TV)

wines.name: Col Vetoraz Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: The entry Brut Valdobbiadene DOCG from Col Vetoraz. The estate's altitude above Santo Stefano produces a distinctly cool-climate Glera with sharper acidity and finer aromatics than lower-valley peers.

pairings.why: Acidity and clean fruit balance the tomato's natural acidity beautifully.
Fine effervescence cuts through the milky richness of fresh buffalo mozzarella.
Brut minerality resonates with the saline mussel liquor.

history.summary: Alongside the flagship Cartizze, Col Vetoraz produces a range of Valdobbiadene DOCG expressions. The standard Brut represents the estate's house style at accessible pricing: elevation-driven acidity and mineral character that sets it apart from lower valley production.

milestones.event: Col Vetoraz launches the Valdobbiadene DOCG Brut alongside the Cartizze at founding.

wines.description: Col Vetoraz's Brut Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG. The estate's elevated position above Santo Stefano produces a cooler-climate Glera with sharper acidity and mineral freshness.

verified.address_quoted: Loc. Col Vetoraz 1, 31040 Santo Stefano di Valdobbiadene (TV)

wines.name: Col Vetoraz Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: The Extra Dry expression from Col Vetoraz's elevated hilltop estate. Off-dry with gentle floral sweetness; the altitude of the estate prevents the Extra Dry from tipping into overripe territory, maintaining freshness.

pairings.why: Off-dry sweetness counterbalances the saltiness of cured meats.
Light sweetness and fine mousse complement the delicate filling.

history.summary: The Extra Dry was added to the Col Vetoraz range a few years after founding, responding to the category's dominance in Italian aperitivo culture. As with the Brut, the estate's altitude at Santo Stefano gives the Extra Dry a lift of freshness unusual in the style.

milestones.event: Col Vetoraz adds Extra Dry to the range alongside the Brut and Cartizze.

wines.description: Col Vetoraz's Extra Dry Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG. Altitude-influenced freshness stops the off-dry sweetness from tipping into overripe; ripe pear and elderflower with a citrus lift.

verified.address_quoted: Loc. Col Vetoraz 1, 31040 Santo Stefano di Valdobbiadene (TV)

wines.name: Foss Marai Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: The house Brut from Foss Marai at Guia di Valdobbiadene. The Guia area has some of the steepest terraced vineyards in the DOCG zone; the Brut reflects this with a crisp, mineral precision.

pairings.why: Mineral and high-acid brut is a clean match for mixed chilled seafood.
Crisp acidity amplifies the saline depth of clam pasta.

history.summary: Foss Marai was founded in 1947 at Guia di Valdobbiadene, one of the steepest sub-zones within the DOCG. The estate's founder chose Guia for its south-facing terraces on chalk and clay soils. The Brut has been the house standard since the beginning.

milestones.event: Foss Marai founded at Guia di Valdobbiadene on steep terraced hillsides.

wines.description: Foss Marai's house Brut from Guia di Valdobbiadene. The steep terraced vineyards in this sub-zone give the Prosecco Superiore DOCG a mineral crispness and precise acidity.

wines.name: Foss Marai Drusian Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: Drusian is the flagship Extra Dry from Foss Marai, named after the locality of the Guia vineyards. Softer and more aromatic than the Brut, with white peach and acacia blossom in an off-dry, easy-drinking style.

pairings.why: Off-dry softness contrasts the saltiness of air-dried beef and peppery rocket.
Creamy salmon spread is lifted by the wine's gentle sweetness and effervescence.

history.summary: The Drusian Extra Dry was developed to give Foss Marai a presence in the Extra Dry category that dominates Italian domestic and export Prosecco sales. Named for the Drusian area of the Guia sub-zone, it became the estate's best-selling label.

milestones.event: Foss Marai launches Drusian Extra Dry as the flagship accessible label.

wines.description: Foss Marai's flagship Extra Dry Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG, named for the Drusian area of the Guia sub-zone. Soft white peach and acacia with gentle off-dry sweetness.

wines.name: Foss Marai Gran Cuvee Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut
wines.oak_regime: No oak; extended Charmat autolysis

taste.summary: Gran Cuvee uses extended Charmat contact to develop yeast-derived bread and cream complexity in the Prosecco Superiore framework. A premium brut with more depth than the house standard Brut.

pairings.why: Bread and cream notes in the wine complement the earthiness of mushroom risotto.
Yeast-derived complexity of Gran Cuvee stands up to a rich bisque.

history.summary: Gran Cuvee was introduced as Foss Marai's premium expression below the Cartizze category, employing a longer stay in the pressurised Charmat tanks to develop autolytic notes. It bridges the gap between the accessible Drusian Extra Dry and a premium sparkling wine experience.

milestones.event: Foss Marai launches Gran Cuvee as an extended-autolysis premium Brut Valdobbiadene DOCG.

wines.description: Foss Marai's premium Brut Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG. Gran Cuvee uses extended Charmat autolysis for bread, cream and biscuit complexity beyond the standard range.

wines.name: Bele Casel Asolo Prosecco Superiore Extra Dry
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: Asolo Prosecco DOCG is produced on the south-facing slopes of the Asolo hills, southwest of Valdobbiadene. Bele Casel uses indigenous Perera and Verdiso alongside Glera for a more complex aromatic profile than monovarietal Prosecco.

pairings.why: Off-dry softness contrasts the crisp coating while the floral notes echo the fresh fish.
Classic Italian pairing: the wine's off-dry fruit and the sweet melon are natural companions.
Light sweetness and fine effervescence lift the creamy cheese.

history.summary: Bele Casel was founded in 1968 at Caerano di San Marco in the Asolo hills. The estate has maintained the use of native varieties including Perera and Verdiso alongside Glera, preserving the historical field-blend tradition of the Asolo zone before monovarietal Glera became dominant.

milestones.event: Bele Casel founded; Glera, Perera and Verdiso planted for Asolo DOCG production.
Asolo Prosecco granted DOCG status, elevating the zone above the broader DOC.

wines.description: Bele Casel's Asolo Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry. Indigenous Perera and Verdiso alongside Glera add aromatic complexity to this off-dry Asolo hills expression.

verified.address_quoted: Via Cascin 4, 31030 Caerano di San Marco (TV)

wines.name: Bele Casel Col Fondo Asolo Prosecco
wines.oak_regime: No oak; ancestral method (col fondo = with sediment)

taste.summary: Col Fondo (with sediment) is the traditional method of making Prosecco before the Charmat process became dominant. Bele Casel's version undergoes secondary fermentation in bottle, leaving the lees (col fondo), resulting in a cloudy, autolytic character that is the antithesis of the commercial tank-method style.

pairings.dish: Venetian sarde in saor (sweet and sour sardines)
pairings.why: The textural yeast and saline mineral of col fondo resonates with the sweet-sour brine of sardines.
The wine's yeasty complexity stands up to a nutty aged Asiago.
The wine's mineral and acidic lift cuts through the richness of salt cod preparations.

history.summary: The col fondo style predates Charmat-method Prosecco and was how all Prosecco was made until the early 20th century. Bele Casel revived a dedicated col fondo bottling as interest in natural and traditional methods grew. Using the ancestral method with Glera and Verdiso, the wine referments in bottle rather than in a sealed tank.

milestones.event: Bele Casel releases dedicated col fondo (ancestral method) bottling for the natural wine market.

wines.description: Bele Casel's ancestral-method col fondo Prosecco. Bottle-refermented with lees contact, producing a cloudy, yeasty, mineral expression of Glera and Verdiso in the traditional Prosecco style.

verified.address_quoted: Via Cascin 4, 31030 Caerano di San Marco (TV)

wines.name: Bele Casel Costalunga Asolo Prosecco Superiore Brut
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: Costalunga is Bele Casel's single-vineyard Brut from the Costalunga slope within the Asolo DOCG zone. The steep south-facing aspect and altitude produce a more concentrated and mineral brut than the entry-level Extra Dry.

pairings.why: Mineral acidity and clean fruit accent the delicate butter-and-lemon sauce.
The wine's fresh herb notes echo the mint in the soup while acidity lifts the sweetness of peas.

history.summary: Costalunga is named for a specific slope within the Asolo hills where Bele Casel cultivates a separate selection of Glera for this higher-quality Brut. The name means 'long slope' in the local dialect, referencing the extended terraced vineyards of the Asolo zone.

milestones.event: Bele Casel introduces Costalunga as a single-slope premium Brut Asolo DOCG.

wines.description: Bele Casel's single-slope Asolo Prosecco Superiore DOCG Brut from the Costalunga terraced hillside. Mineral, crisp and clean; more concentrated than the entry Extra Dry.

verified.address_quoted: Via Cascin 4, 31030 Caerano di San Marco (TV)

wines.name: Case Paolin Asolo Prosecco Superiore Extra Brut
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: Case Paolin's signature expression from the Montello foothills in the Asolo DOCG zone. The Giusto family uses indigenous varieties including Verdiso and Perera for complexity, and favours a lower dosage Extra Brut style for a drier, mineral-driven Asolo Prosecco Superiore.

pairings.why: Briny oysters demand the driest, most mineral Prosecco; Extra Brut fits perfectly.
The wine's mineral-green thread echoes asparagus while acidity provides contrast.
Racy acidity and citrus character lift delicate raw tuna.

history.summary: Case Paolin was established in 1987 by the Giusto family at the foot of Montello in the Asolo zone. The estate's philosophy centres on lower-dosage styles and the use of native Veneto varieties alongside Glera to distinguish Asolo DOCG from the better-known Valdobbiadene. The Extra Brut label is their commitment to the driest style of the zone.

milestones.event: Case Paolin founded; Extra Brut chosen as the house's signature Asolo DOCG expression.
Asolo Prosecco DOCG elevation; Case Paolin's Extra Brut benefits from the new classification.

wines.description: Case Paolin's lower-dosage Asolo Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Brut. Indigenous Verdiso and Perera alongside Glera produce a mineral, dry and refreshing expression from the Montello foothills.

verified.address_quoted: Via Case Paolin 5, 31030 Volpago del Montello (TV)

wines.name: Case Paolin Millesimato Asolo Prosecco Superiore Brut
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: Case Paolin's vintage-dated Millesimato (single-year harvest) Brut expresses the character of a single season from the Montello-Asolo hills. Only released in years when harvest quality justifies the vintage designation.

pairings.why: The wine's mineral character and clean acidity complement truffle's earthy richness without competing.
Delicate fish needs the precision acidity and stone-fruit note of the Millesimato.

history.summary: The Millesimato format (vintage-dated) allows Case Paolin to express exceptional individual harvests from the Asolo DOCG zone. Introduced in 2000 to showcase the quality potential of the Montello-Asolo hills, it is only bottled in years with exceptional fruit quality.

milestones.event: Case Paolin first releases Millesimato as a vintage-dated single-harvest Asolo DOCG Brut.

wines.description: Case Paolin's vintage-dated Asolo Prosecco Superiore DOCG Millesimato Brut. Released only in exceptional years to showcase the Montello-Asolo terroir character.

verified.address_quoted: Via Case Paolin 5, 31030 Volpago del Montello (TV)

wines.name: Serafini and Vidotto Il Rosso dell'Abazia
wines.oak_regime: 18-24 months in small French oak barriques, percentage new varies by vintage

taste.summary: The flagship of the Montello DOCG, one of northeastern Italy's most serious Bordeaux-blend reds. Serafini and Vidotto pioneered quality Bordeaux-variety production on the Montello hills in the 1980s when the zone was considered secondary. Il Rosso dell'Abazia has become the reference point for the designation.

pairings.dish: Brasato al Barolo (beef braised in red wine)
pairings.why: The wine's firm structure and dark fruit are a classic match for slow-braised beef.
Structured tannins and acidity cut through the crystalline fat of aged Montasio.
Cedar and tobacco notes in the wine complement the herb-crusted lamb beautifully.

history.summary: Francesco Serafini and Antonello Vidotto established their Nervesa della Battaglia estate in 1986 to produce premium Bordeaux-variety wines on the Montello hills above the Piave river. Il Rosso dell'Abazia (the abbey's red wine) references the historic Abbazia di Nervesa monastery whose dissolution in 1806 released the estate lands for viticulture. First released in 1990, it established Montello as a serious zone for ageable reds.

milestones.event: Serafini and Vidotto found the Nervesa della Battaglia estate.
First release of Il Rosso dell'Abazia from the Montello hillside vineyards.
Montello Rosso elevated to DOCG, recognising the zone's capacity for premium red wines.

wines.description: The benchmark red of Montello Rosso DOCG from Serafini and Vidotto. Bordeaux varieties grown on the Montello hills produce a structured, ageable wine with blackcurrant, cedar and firm tannins.

verified.address_quoted: Via Ca' di Serra 2, 31044 Nervesa della Battaglia (TV)

wines.name: Serafini and Vidotto Phigaia
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in a mix of small and medium oak

taste.summary: Phigaia is the accessible second wine from Serafini and Vidotto, allowing the estate to express the Montello terroir at a lower price point. The Cabernet Franc and Merlot blend is lighter, fresher and more immediately enjoyable than Il Rosso dell'Abazia.

pairings.why: Medium-bodied red with good acidity is a natural match for fresh pasta and meat ragu.
Herby, medium-tannin red pairs naturally with tomato and mozzarella.
Medium structure and red fruit handle the mild pork-and-polenta combination elegantly.

history.summary: Phigaia was developed as a younger, more accessible expression from the Serafini and Vidotto Montello estate. While Il Rosso dell'Abazia ages in barrel for up to two years, Phigaia uses a shorter regimen for a fresher, more fruit-forward profile from the same Bordeaux varieties.

milestones.event: Phigaia introduced as the accessible second wine of Serafini and Vidotto.

wines.description: The accessible second wine from Serafini and Vidotto's Montello estate. Phigaia blends Cabernet Franc and Merlot for a lighter, immediately enjoyable Treviso IGT red.

verified.address_quoted: Via Ca' di Serra 2, 31044 Nervesa della Battaglia (TV)

wines.name: Serafini and Vidotto Bollicine di Prosecco
wines.oak_regime: No oak; Charmat method

taste.summary: The Prosecco DOC entry point from the Montello red-wine estate. Serafini and Vidotto produces Bollicine di Prosecco from Treviso-zone Glera as an accessible sparkling alongside the estate's main red wine mission.

pairings.why: Accessible, clean sparkling works across a range of light starters.
Standard off-dry Extra Dry style, ideal for informal aperitivo occasions.

history.summary: Serafini and Vidotto expanded into Prosecco DOC production to complement the estate's red wine focus. Bollicine (bubbles) is the informal, everyday sparkling from the Trevigiana area outside the DOCG hills.

milestones.event: Serafini and Vidotto adds Bollicine di Prosecco DOC Treviso to the portfolio.

wines.description: Serafini and Vidotto's organic Prosecco DOC Treviso from the Montello estate. Bollicine di Prosecco is a clean, accessible off-dry sparkling as a counterpoint to the estate's main red wine range.

verified.address_quoted: Via Ca' di Serra 2, 31044 Nervesa della Battaglia (TV)

wines.name: Guerrieri Rizzardi Bardolino Classico
wines.oak_regime: Short period in large Slavonian oak, then stainless steel

taste.summary: A classic light-bodied Bardolino Classico from the Lake Garda shoreline estates. The Classico denomination requires production from the original Bardolino historic core. Guerrieri Rizzardi, as one of the oldest estates in the zone, produces a benchmark expression of the pale ruby, cherry-scented style.

pairings.why: Light-bodied Bardolino is one of Italy's classic red wines with freshwater fish.
Easy tannins and cherry fruit make this an ideal pizza companion.
The local cured beef speciality and Bardolino Classico are a regional pairing tradition.

history.summary: Guerrieri Rizzardi is one of Italy's oldest documented wine estates, with vineyard records from 1649. The Bardolino Classico is the estate's expression of the Lake Garda lakefront appellation that surrounds the historic Bardolino town. The light, pale ruby style is characteristic of the sandy moraine soils along the eastern Garda shore.

milestones.event: Guerrieri Rizzardi records earliest documented vineyard production in Bardolino.
Bardolino Superiore elevated to DOCG, with the Classico sub-zone retaining DOC.

wines.description: Classic Lake Garda red from one of Bardolino's oldest estates. Light ruby, sour cherry and herb in a refreshing, low-tannin style that is the region's classic companion to fish and casual meals.

wines.name: Guerrieri Rizzardi Tacchetto Bardolino Chiaretto Classico
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel for freshness

taste.summary: Tacchetto is a single-vineyard Bardolino Chiaretto from the estate's oldest Corvina vineyard. Chiaretto (meaning 'light' or 'pale') is the rosato of Garda, made by short skin contact on Corvina, Rondinella and Molinara. Pale salmon-pink with delicate berry and rose aromatics.

pairings.why: Bardolino Chiaretto is the quintessential companion to Garda's lake perch.
Pale rosato's delicate tannins and fresh acidity contrast the richness of raw beef.
The wine's strawberry-tomato register and acidity align with a fresh caprese.

history.summary: Tacchetto is a vineyard name within the Guerrieri Rizzardi estate at Bardolino. The Chiaretto style was historically produced throughout the Garda area as the 'rosato' of the region, made by bleeding off juice from red grapes after a brief skin contact. The Classico designation restricts production to the historic Bardolino core.

milestones.event: Guerrieri Rizzardi releases Tacchetto as a single-vineyard Bardolino Chiaretto Classico DOC.

wines.description: A single-vineyard Bardolino Chiaretto Classico from Guerrieri Rizzardi. Pale salmon-pink with wild strawberry and rose petal; the quintessential Lake Garda rosato for summer and lake fish.

wines.name: Guerrieri Rizzardi Pojega Bardolino Superiore
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in Slavonian oak botti

taste.summary: Pojega is the top expression from Guerrieri Rizzardi's Bardolino range, bottled under the Bardolino Superiore DOCG. Oak ageing adds structure and complexity to the base Classico profile. Named for the Pojega wine museum located on the estate.

pairings.why: Medium body and structured acidity complement the richness of duck and cornmeal.
Savoury tannins and tart cherry pair naturally with sliced grilled beef.

pairings.dish: Cotechino con lenticchie (pork sausage with lentils)
pairings.why: Structured, dry Bardolino Superiore matches the hearty north Italian winter dish.

history.summary: The Bardolino Superiore DOCG was established in 2001 as the premium tier of Bardolino, requiring a minimum 12-month ageing. Guerrieri Rizzardi named their DOCG expression Pojega after the Pojega wine museum on the estate, one of Italy's longest-running wine museums open since 1975.

milestones.event: Bardolino Superiore elevated to DOCG; Guerrieri Rizzardi releases Pojega as the DOCG flagship.

wines.description: Guerrieri Rizzardi's Bardolino Superiore DOCG flagship from the Pojega vineyard. Oak-aged Corvina blend with structured acidity, savoury herbs and cherry; the most serious red in the Bardolino zone.

wines.name: Guerrieri Rizzardi Villa Rizzardi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: 3 years in large Slavonian oak botti

taste.summary: Guerrieri Rizzardi's Amarone expresses the Classico zone from an estate with over 350 years of Valpolicella history. The extended appassimento (drying) of Corvina and Corvinone grapes concentrates flavour into a wine of great intensity. Three years in Slavonian botti soften the tannins while preserving the dried-fruit character.

pairings.why: The classic regional pairing: using the same wine in the sauce as in the glass.
The wine's tannic structure and dried fruit character are amplified by the crystalline salt of aged Parmesan.
The chocolate and espresso notes in Amarone create a natural affinity with dark chocolate desserts.

history.summary: Although the Guerrieri Rizzardi estate dates from 1649, the modern Amarone label was established in the late 1960s when Amarone became commercially recognised. Villa Rizzardi Amarone is produced from the estate's Valpolicella Classico vineyards, where grapes undergo traditional appassimento for 90-120 days before pressing.

milestones.event: Guerrieri Rizzardi begins commercial Amarone production under the Villa Rizzardi label.
Amarone della Valpolicella elevated to DOCG from DOC.

wines.description: Guerrieri Rizzardi's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG from a 350-year-old Bardolino and Valpolicella estate. Appassimento drying concentrates flavour into intense dried cherry, dark chocolate and tobacco.

wines.name: Cavalchina Bianco di Custoza
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel with controlled temperature

taste.summary: The Piona family's benchmark Bianco di Custoza. The DOC requires a minimum of five grape varieties, and Cavalchina's multi-varietal blend from morainic glacial soils south of Lake Garda delivers a crisp, food-friendly white with genuine complexity from the variety mix.

pairings.why: Crisp acidity and mineral freshness are the natural partner for the region's lake fish.
The wine's floral and citrus notes echo green herbs in a light risotto.
Light acidity and mineral freshness complement grilled vegetables without overwhelming.

history.summary: Cavalchina at Sommacampagna was one of the pioneer estates at the Bianco di Custoza DOC's inception in 1971. The morainic amphitheatre south of Lake Garda is a glacially deposited mix of different soils ideal for white wine blends. The Piona family has championed the multi-varietal Custoza style since the DOC's foundation.

milestones.event: Bianco di Custoza DOC established; Cavalchina among the founding producers.
Cavalchina officially launched as an estate; first Bianco di Custoza DOC production.

wines.description: The benchmark Bianco di Custoza DOC from the Piona family estate at Sommacampagna. Five-variety blend from morainic soils south of Lake Garda; crisp, mineral and versatile with lake fish.

wines.name: Cavalchina Amedeo Bianco di Custoza DOC
wines.oak_regime: Partial fermentation in new oak barriques

taste.summary: Amedeo is Cavalchina's premium selection, using partial oak fermentation to add complexity and longevity to the Bianco di Custoza DOC framework. More structured and richer than the standard Custoza, with the oak well integrated.

pairings.why: The wine's oak-influenced richness and cream texture are ideal with roasted poultry.
Structured white wine complements the delicate flavours of filled pasta in broth.
The wine's aromatics amplify the anise note of fennel and the richness of a grilled whole fish.

history.summary: Amedeo was introduced to offer a premium oak-influenced tier to the Custoza range, addressing demand for a more complex, age-worthy Bianco di Custoza. Named for Amedeo Piona, a previous generation of the founding family, it uses the same DOC blend but with a portion fermented in new barriques.
