milestones.event: Cavalchina introduces Amedeo as a premium oak-fermented Bianco di Custoza DOC.

wines.description: Cavalchina's premium oak-fermented Bianco di Custoza DOC. Amedeo uses partial new barriques to add cream, vanilla and structure to the Custoza multi-varietal blend.

wines.name: Cavalchina Bardolino Chiaretto Classico
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel cold fermentation

taste.summary: Cavalchina's Chiaretto Classico extends the estate's Lake Garda portfolio from white to rosato. Brief skin contact on Corvina, Rondinella and Molinara produces a pale copper-pink wine with fresh berry and rose aromatics; dry and food-versatile.

pairings.why: The classic Garda pairing: pale Chiaretto with the region's prized lake fish.
Light body and refreshing acidity suit warm-weather pasta dishes.
Dry, mineral rosato complements the slightly charred, earthy character of grilled vegetable antipasto.

history.summary: Cavalchina's Chiaretto draws on the estate's proximity to the Bardolino Classico zone along the eastern Garda shore. Though Cavalchina is primarily known for Bianco di Custoza, the Chiaretto expresses the estate's dual Lake Garda heritage from glacial moraine soils.

milestones.event: Cavalchina launches a Bardolino Chiaretto Classico DOC to complement the Custoza whites.

wines.description: Cavalchina's Bardolino Chiaretto Classico DOC. Pale copper-pink with fresh strawberry and rose petal; dry and mineral with crisp acidity for summer drinking and lake fish.

wines.name: Maculan Torcolato Breganze
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in new and used French oak barriques

taste.summary: Italy's most celebrated passito-style dessert wine from Vicenza. Vespaiola grapes are harvested late and dried on twisted cords (torcolato means 'twisted') for months before pressing. The result is a wine of remarkable intensity, with botrytis-influenced apricot honey character balanced by the grape's naturally high acidity.

pairings.why: Honey and apricot in the wine are amplified by almond pastry without becoming cloying.
Classic sweet-salty pairing: the wine's sweetness contrasts with pungent blue cheese.
The traditional Sauternes-foie gras match applies equally to Torcolato's richness.

history.summary: Fausto Maculan revived Torcolato as a serious dessert wine in 1974, rescuing a traditional Breganze style that had nearly disappeared. The Vespaiola grape was grown historically in the Vicenza hills and dried on twisted ropes (torcolati) to concentrate sugar. Maculan's Torcolato established the wine as Italy's answer to Sauternes and inspired the formal Breganze Torcolato DOC.

milestones.event: Fausto Maculan releases the first modern Torcolato, reviving the near-extinct Breganze passito tradition.
Breganze Torcolato recognised as a specific DOC sub-type within the Breganze DOC.

wines.description: Italy's most celebrated passito-style dessert wine. Fausto Maculan revived the Vespaiola dried-grape tradition at Breganze in 1974 to produce a Torcolato of remarkable honey, apricot and botrytis complexity.

wines.name: Maculan Acininobili Breganze
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in new French oak barriques

taste.summary: One of Italy's greatest botrytised dessert wines. Acininobili (noble berries) is produced from 100% botrytis-affected Vespaiola grapes, selected berry by berry in the best vintages. The Sauternes influence is explicit: Maculan was inspired by a trip to Bordeaux in the late 1970s and applied the Trockenbeerenauslese selection principle to Vespaiola.

pairings.why: The quintessential botrytis-wine pairing: the wine's sweetness and acidity cut through the rich liver.
Caramelised vanilla custard harmonises with the wine's honey, vanilla and caramel notes.
Noble rot dessert wine and blue cheese is a classic high-contrast pairing.

history.summary: After reviving Torcolato in 1974, Fausto Maculan pushed further by producing Italy's first serious botrytised dessert wine from Vespaiola in 1979. Inspired by Chateau d'Yquem and Trockenbeerenauslese, Acininobili uses only botrytis-infected berries selected by hand in exceptional years. It is not produced in every vintage.

milestones.event: Maculan produces the first Acininobili from botrytis-selected Vespaiola grapes.
Acininobili wins international acclaim in comparative Sauternes tastings.

wines.description: Italy's foremost botrytised dessert wine. Maculan's Acininobili uses hand-selected Vespaiola grapes attacked by noble rot, producing a Sauternes-inspired wine of extraordinary complexity and longevity.

wines.name: Maculan Fratta Breganze Rosso
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in new and second-year French oak barriques

taste.summary: Fratta is Maculan's Bordeaux-inspired flagship red, one of northeastern Italy's landmark wines. A Cabernet-Merlot blend aged 18 months in new French oak, it rivals top Veneto reds in complexity and ageing potential. Fausto Maculan was among the first producers in the Veneto to vinify Bordeaux varieties at serious quality levels.

pairings.why: The wine's firm structure and dark fruit are ideal for slow-braised beef with acidic gremolata contrast.
Game and firm tannins are a natural pairing; polenta cushions the structure.
Crystalline salt of aged Parmesan amplifies the wine's dark fruit and mineral notes.

history.summary: Fratta (meaning 'thicket' or 'scrubland') was Maculan's first serious red wine, launched in 1977 when Bordeaux-variety viticulture in the Veneto was almost unheard of. The wine was initially bottled as Breganze Cabernet, later reformulated as a Cabernet-Merlot blend. It helped establish the possibility of serious red wine production in Breganze alongside the internationally acclaimed dessert wines.

milestones.event: Maculan first releases Fratta as a Bordeaux-variety red from the Breganze DOC.
Fratta reformulated with Merlot blended alongside Cabernet Sauvignon.

wines.description: Maculan's Bordeaux-variety flagship red, first produced in 1977. Fratta is a Cabernet-Merlot blend aged 18 months in new French oak; one of northeastern Italy's most ageable reds.

wines.name: Maculan Dindarello Breganze
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel cold fermentation

taste.summary: Dindarello is Maculan's late-harvest Moscato, a lighter and more delicate counterpart to the intense Torcolato and Acininobili. From small Moscato (Muscat blanc a petits grains) grapes left on the vine to develop extra ripeness, it captures the variety's natural floral sweetness without botrytis intervention.

pairings.why: The wine's floral sweetness echoes and amplifies the natural fruit sugars.
Delicate sweetness and floral character harmonise with sponge and fresh berries.
Orange blossom and peach notes in the wine mirror the custard and stone fruit.

history.summary: Dindarello completes Maculan's dessert wine trilogy at the lighter, more accessible end. Moscato grows around Breganze's lower-altitude slopes and the variety's natural aromatic intensity translates readily into a charming late-harvest sweet wine. The name is a local dialect term for a bell sound.

milestones.event: Maculan launches Dindarello as a late-harvest Moscato from the Breganze DOC area.

wines.description: Maculan's late-harvest Moscato from Breganze. Dindarello is the lightest and most floral of the Maculan dessert wine range: orange blossom, peach and rose in a delicate sweet style.

wines.name: Maculan Pino and Toi Breganze Bianco
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel cold fermentation

taste.summary: Pino and Toi (Pine and You in Veneto dialect) is Maculan's accessible dry white, a refreshing blend of the two Pinots and Tocai for everyday drinking. It represents the lighter, approachable side of the Breganze DOC in contrast to the estate's intense dessert wines.

pairings.why: Breganze white wine is traditionally served alongside this Vicenza specialty.

pairings.dish: Grilled chicken with lemon and herbs
pairings.why: Clean, medium-bodied white works well with herb-marinated poultry.
Versatile dry white suitable for a range of light antipasto preparations.

history.summary: Pino and Toi was introduced as Maculan's everyday white wine, a blend of varieties traditionally grown in the Vicenza hills. The playful name and accessible price point are designed to complement the prestige Torcolato and Acininobili, making the estate accessible to a wider audience.

milestones.event: Maculan launches Pino and Toi as an everyday dry white Breganze DOC.

wines.description: Maculan's accessible dry white from Breganze DOC. Pino and Toi blends Pinot Grigio, Pinot Bianco and Tocai for a clean, everyday white with orchard fruit and mineral freshness.

wines.name: Vignalta Colli Euganei Fior d'Arancio Passito
wines.oak_regime: Aged in small oak and stainless steel after fermentation on dried skins

taste.summary: The defining wine of the Colli Euganei Fior d'Arancio DOCG, Italy's most aromatic dessert wine appellation. Moscato Giallo grapes are dried (passito) on bamboo racks in the volcanic Euganean Hills; the intensely floral and orange-scented result is unlike any other Italian sweet wine.

pairings.why: The regional tradition of dunking almond biscuits in a sweet, aromatic wine applies perfectly.
The wine's floral orange character creates a seamless echo with the dessert.
Sweet wine and aged hard cheese is a classic Italian pairing; the orange blossom cuts through the crystalline fat.

history.summary: Vignalta has been the benchmark producer of Colli Euganei Fior d'Arancio since the DOCG's establishment. The Euganean Hills (Colli Euganei) are volcanic, rising steeply from the Padovan plain south of Padua. Moscato Giallo has been grown here for centuries for its compatibility with the volcanic soil and warm microclimate. The passito style (dried grape) was formalised with the creation of the DOCG in 2010.

milestones.event: Vignalta first produces a Fior d'Arancio Passito from dried Moscato Giallo on the Euganean Hills.
Colli Euganei Fior d'Arancio elevated to DOCG.

wines.description: The defining passito wine of the Colli Euganei Fior d'Arancio DOCG. Dried Moscato Giallo from Vignalta's volcanic Euganean Hills vineyards produces Italy's most aromatic dessert wine: orange blossom, candied peel and concentrated honey.

wines.name: Vignalta Gemola Colli Euganei
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak barriques, mix new and used

taste.summary: Gemola is the flagship red of Vignalta and one of the Veneto's most distinctive non-Valpolicella reds. The volcanic basalt soils of the Euganean Hills give a distinctive mineral thread to this Merlot-Cabernet Franc blend that is unlike the softer alluvial reds of the plain.

pairings.why: Firm tannins and cedar-herbal notes complement herb-roasted lamb.
Cherry fruit in the wine mirrors the sauce while structure handles the rich duck fat.
Mineral and herbal thread of the wine echo the earthy depth of mushrooms.

history.summary: Vignalta was founded in 1980 but only began producing Gemola from the estate's best volcanic parcels in 1989. The Euganean Hills' basaltic and trachytic volcanic soils give a distinctive mineral character to red wines that distinguishes them from the more famous Valpolicella and Soave zones. Gemola has become the reference red wine of this often-overlooked volcanic appellation.

milestones.event: Vignalta first releases Gemola as a Merlot-Cabernet Franc blend from the volcanic Euganean Hills.

wines.description: Vignalta's flagship red from the volcanic Euganean Hills. Gemola blends Merlot and Cabernet Franc from basalt soils for a structured, mineral red unlike typical Veneto production.

wines.name: Vignalta Cabernet Colli Euganei Rosso
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French oak

taste.summary: A Cabernet-focused red from the volcanic slopes of the Euganean Hills. The Colli Euganei DOC allows Cabernet varieties and Vignalta's version shows the distinctive mineral thread of volcanic soil combined with the classic Cabernet Franc herbal character.

pairings.why: Firm tannins and dark fruit are the classic pairing for grilled red meat.
Herbal Cabernet Franc notes complement the herbs in a slow-cooked stew.

history.summary: Vignalta began cultivating Cabernet varieties on the Euganean Hills in the early 1980s, recognising that the volcanic basalt soils suited Bordeaux varieties. The standard Cabernet expression predates Gemola and serves as the estate's accessible red entry point for the Colli Euganei DOC.

milestones.event: Vignalta releases first Cabernet from the Colli Euganei DOC from volcanic basalt plots.

wines.description: Vignalta's Cabernet-focused red from the Colli Euganei DOC. Volcanic basalt soils give a mineral thread to the classic Cabernet Franc herbal and dark fruit character.

wines.name: Vignalta Pinot Bianco Colli Euganei
wines.oak_regime: No oak; temperature-controlled stainless steel

taste.summary: Pinot Bianco on the volcanic soils of the Euganean Hills produces a wine of notable mineral freshness. The volcanic basalt structure of the Colli Euganei translates into a flinty mineral quality in white wines rarely seen in the Veneto plains.

pairings.why: Mineral acidity and clean fruit are the classic accompaniment to mixed fried seafood.
Light, fresh white wine complements the delicate sweetness of courgette risotto.

history.summary: Pinot Bianco has been grown in the Euganean Hills for over a century. Vignalta produces it as a single-varietal expression from volcanic parcels, emphasising the mineral character the basalt soils impart. It serves as the estate's dry white counterpart to the aromatic Fior d'Arancio and the full-bodied Gemola red.

milestones.event: Vignalta first releases a single-varietal Pinot Bianco from volcanic Colli Euganei parcels.

wines.description: Vignalta's Pinot Bianco from volcanic Colli Euganei DOC. Basalt soils give a mineral thread and white peach clarity to this crisp dry white.

wines.name: Italo Cescon Raboso Piave DOC
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in Slavonian oak botti

taste.summary: Raboso Piave is one of Italy's most ancient and austere red grapes, native to the Piave river plains in Treviso province. Italo Cescon has championed its rehabilitation since the 1960s. High tannin and acid give the wine its characteristic austerity, but serious age-worthiness.

pairings.dish: Stufato di manzo con polenta (beef stew with polenta)
pairings.why: The hearty Treviso combination of slow-cooked beef and polenta demands a rustic, structured red like Raboso.
Tannin and acidity are tamed by the fat and salt of aged cheese.
Gamey richness requires a wine of equivalent structure and grip.

history.summary: Raboso Piave is documented in the Treviso plains since Roman times and was long considered an unrefined workhorse grape. Italo Cescon's advocacy and careful winemaking since the 1960s helped reframe Raboso as a genuine, ageable autochthonous variety worthy of a DOC designation. The Piave DOC's Raboso sub-type was established to protect and regulate its production.

milestones.event: Italo Cescon founds the estate and begins producing Raboso Piave as a serious varietal wine.
Piave DOC established; Raboso Piave becomes a recognised sub-variety within the appellation.

wines.description: Italy's most ancient and austere red grape: Raboso Piave from Italo Cescon's Treviso plain estate. High tannin and acid demand food pairing but reward cellaring with complexity.

wines.name: Italo Cescon Il Tralcetto Pinot Grigio delle Venezie
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel cold fermentation

taste.summary: The volume accessible white from Italo Cescon. Pinot Grigio delle Venezie DOC is the broad appellation for Pinot Grigio from the Tre Venezie; the Cescon version is produced from Piave valley floor vineyards and represents the commercial, food-versatile tier of the estate alongside the character-driven Raboso.

pairings.why: Clean, neutral Pinot Grigio is the universal match for simple grilled sea bass.
Crisp acidity enhances the brine of clams in a minimal pasta.

history.summary: Il Tralcetto (meaning 'little tendril') represents the entry-level commercial production from Italo Cescon, providing the estate with accessible volume alongside the more distinctive Raboso. The Pinot Grigio delle Venezie DOC is a large appellation encompassing the Tre Venezie (Veneto, Friuli-Venezia Giulia, Trentino-Alto Adige).

milestones.event: Italo Cescon launches Il Tralcetto as the accessible Pinot Grigio tier of the estate range.

wines.description: Italo Cescon's accessible Pinot Grigio delle Venezie DOC. Il Tralcetto is clean, crisp and food-versatile; a commercial counterpart to the estate's distinctive Raboso Piave.

wines.name: Italo Cescon Merlot Piave DOC
wines.oak_regime: 6 months in Slavonian oak botti

taste.summary: The Piave plain Merlot DOC from Italo Cescon is a classic Trevisan everyday red. Merlot on the alluvial Piave soils produces a plum-forward, medium-bodied wine with soft tannins suited to simple food pairings.

pairings.why: The quintessential Veneto combination: medium-bodied red with grilled pork sausages and white polenta.
Soft Merlot with medium acidity is a classic pizza-and-pasta wine.

history.summary: Merlot has been planted in the Piave DOC zone since the late 19th century, brought by Venetian nobles who recognised its affinity with the alluvial soils of the plains. Italo Cescon has produced Piave Merlot DOC since the 1970s as part of the estate's range alongside Raboso.

milestones.event: Piave DOC established; Merlot recognised as a key variety within the appellation.
Italo Cescon formalises Merlot Piave DOC production.

wines.description: Italo Cescon's Piave Merlot DOC from Treviso plain vineyards. A classic everyday medium-bodied red with plum fruit and soft tannins for simple Veneto meals.

wines.name: Gorgo Bianco di Custoza DOC
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel cold fermentation

taste.summary: The Gorgo estate at Custoza di Valeggio sul Mincio produces one of the most consistently mineral Bianco di Custoza expressions. The morainic glacial soils on the southern slope of the Lake Garda amphitheatre impart a saline mineral quality to the Garganega-Trebbiano-Cortese blend.

pairings.dish: Pesce di lago alla griglia (grilled lake fish)
pairings.why: Classic Custoza DOC and lake fish pairing from the Garda region.
Mineral acidity and citrus thread echo and amplify a lemon-accented risotto.
Light mineral white does not compete with truffle but provides a clean counterpoint.

history.summary: Gorgo was founded in 1966 on the glacial moraine south of Lake Garda. The estate's position on the gentle southern slope of the morainic amphitheatre, with its mix of glacially deposited soils, produces a Bianco di Custoza of mineral precision.

milestones.event: Gorgo estate founded at Custoza di Valeggio sul Mincio on glacial moraine soils.
Bianco di Custoza DOC established; Gorgo among founding producers.

wines.description: Gorgo's benchmark Bianco di Custoza DOC from morainic soils south of Lake Garda. A mineral, crisp Garganega-Trebbiano-Cortese blend with notable saline freshness.

verified.address_quoted: Via Gorgo 17, 37067 Custoza di Valeggio sul Mincio (VR)

wines.name: Gorgo Merlot Garda DOC
wines.oak_regime: 6 months in small and medium oak

taste.summary: Gorgo's Garda DOC Merlot provides a red wine counterpart to the estate's primary Bianco di Custoza focus. Lake Garda's moderating effect on the climate produces a well-rounded Merlot with soft tannins and ripe fruit.

pairings.why: Medium-bodied Merlot and sliced steak is a simple, satisfying Garda pairing.
Soft Merlot tannins and ripe fruit complement a simple meat ragu.

history.summary: Gorgo expanded from its white wine focus in the late 1980s and 1990s to produce Garda DOC reds. The Garda DOC appellation covers both white and red wines from the Lake Garda basin. Merlot was chosen for its compatibility with the morainic soils and the temperate lake climate.

milestones.event: Gorgo introduces Merlot Garda DOC to complement the flagship Bianco di Custoza.

wines.description: Gorgo's Garda DOC Merlot from morainic Lake Garda shoreline vineyards. A soft, medium-bodied red with ripe cherry and plum as the estate's red wine complement to the flagship Custoza white.

verified.address_quoted: Via Gorgo 17, 37067 Custoza di Valeggio sul Mincio (VR)

wines.name: Gorgo Bardolino Classico DOC
wines.oak_regime: Short ageing in stainless steel; minimal oak

taste.summary: Gorgo's Bardolino Classico from vineyards along the eastern Garda shore. A light, fresh Corvina-Rondinella red for everyday drinking, complementing the estate's main Custoza white wine focus.

pairings.why: Light Bardolino with freshwater fish is one of Italy's classic regional pairings.
Light red with fresh herb and tomato echoes the flavours of the dish.

history.summary: Gorgo added Bardolino to the range in the mid-1990s to provide a Garda red at the lighter end of the spectrum, complementing the fuller Merlot Garda. Proximity to the Bardolino Classico zone means the estate can source Corvina and Rondinella from the historic core of the appellation.

milestones.event: Gorgo adds Bardolino Classico DOC to the estate range.

wines.description: Gorgo's Bardolino Classico DOC from the Lake Garda shoreline. Light sour cherry and rose petal in an everyday pale red suited to fish and casual meals.

verified.address_quoted: Via Gorgo 17, 37067 Custoza di Valeggio sul Mincio (VR)

wines.name: Ottella Lugana DOC
wines.oak_regime: No oak; temperature-controlled stainless steel

taste.summary: Ottella's standard Lugana DOC is produced from Turbiana (the local name for Verdicchio in the Lugana DOC zone) from estate vineyards at Peschiera del Garda on the southeastern shore of Lake Garda. The clay-limestone soils of the Lugana plain give Turbiana its distinctive almond mineral character.

pairings.why: The regional pairing: Lugana's stone-fruit and almond notes echo in a mushroom risotto made with the same wine.
Classic pairing: Lugana DOC with the lake's freshwater fish is the defining Garda food-and-wine match.
Crisp acidity and mineral freshness cut the batter while respecting the delicate vegetable flavours.

history.summary: The Montresor family founded Ottella in 1980 at Peschiera del Garda, at the southern end of Lake Garda where the Lugana DOC was established in 1967. Turbiana (Trebbiano di Lugana) is the exclusive white grape of the appellation, grown on the heavy clay-limestone soils south of the lake. Ottella specialises exclusively in Lugana and has been among its most consistent quality producers.

milestones.event: Lugana DOC established for Turbiana wines from the southern Lake Garda plains.
Ottella founded at Peschiera del Garda, specialising in Lugana DOC.

wines.description: Ottella's foundational Lugana DOC from Turbiana vineyards at Peschiera del Garda. White peach, almond blossom and mineral chalk; the classic Lake Garda white for freshwater fish and risotto.

wines.name: Ottella Molceo Lugana Riserva DOC
wines.oak_regime: Partial fermentation in large oak; 12+ months on lees in barrel and stainless steel

taste.summary: Molceo is among the most complex white wines produced in the Lugana DOC. Extended lees contact in both oak and steel develops remarkable textural richness and autolytic complexity in the Turbiana grape. One of the first producers to prove that Lugana could produce serious age-worthy whites.

pairings.dish: Seared scallops in lemon butter cream
pairings.why: The wine's rich cream and stone-fruit character mirrors and elevates the buttery scallop sauce.
Rich, complex Riserva demands an equally substantial risotto with shellfish.
Butterscotch and cream notes of the Riserva match hollandaise and the delicate turbot.

history.summary: Molceo is a specific parcel of heavy clay-limestone at the heart of the Ottella estate, identified for its ability to produce Turbiana of exceptional concentration. The Riserva format requires at least 24 months ageing before release; Ottella's version typically ages over a year on lees, pioneering the category of serious long-lived Lugana. Molceo demonstrated that Turbiana could rival international white Burgundy in complexity.

milestones.event: Ottella releases first Molceo Lugana Riserva; extended lees ageing demonstrates category potential.

wines.description: One of the Lugana DOC's finest Riserva wines. Ottella's Molceo uses extended lees contact to develop remarkable cream texture, hazelnut complexity and long mineral persistence from Turbiana.

wines.name: Ottella Le Creete Lugana DOC
wines.oak_regime: No oak; extended lees contact in stainless steel

taste.summary: Le Creete (meaning 'the clay/chalk') is named for the specific Cretaceous clay-limestone soils of the parcel. An intermediate tier between the standard Lugana and the full Riserva Molceo; extended stainless steel lees contact adds texture without the oak of the Riserva.

pairings.why: The wine's stone-fruit and chalk mineral stand up to the smoky richness of trout.
High acidity and light cream texture cut through the tiny fried fish perfectly.

history.summary: Le Creete was introduced to provide a mid-range option between the entry Lugana and the Molceo Riserva. The parcel's heavy Cretaceous clay soil gives the wine its name and its particular mineral precision; lees contact in stainless steel adds texture without the complexity demanded of the Riserva format.

milestones.event: Ottella introduces Le Creete as a clay-parcel single-terroir Lugana DOC between standard and Riserva tiers.

wines.description: Ottella's mid-tier single-parcel Lugana DOC from Cretaceous clay-limestone. Le Creete uses extended lees contact in stainless steel for texture and mineral precision.

wines.name: Corte Gardoni Bianco di Custoza DOC
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel cold fermentation

taste.summary: The Piccoli family's Bianco di Custoza from the morainic rim at Valeggio sul Mincio. The glacially deposited soils on the southern slope of the Lake Garda moraine produce a fresh, food-friendly white with characteristic saline mineral character.

pairings.dish: Poached perch with butter and sage
pairings.why: The Garda region's classic lake fish with a simple butter sauce needs a light, mineral white.
Light acidity and fresh character pair effortlessly with egg-based dishes.

history.summary: Corte Gardoni was established in 1980 by the Piccoli family at Valeggio sul Mincio, a village at the southern tip of the Lake Garda morainic arc. The estate is on the interface between the Bianco di Custoza DOC and the Garda DOC zones, allowing production of both Custoza whites and Garda reds.

milestones.event: Piccoli family founds Corte Gardoni at Valeggio sul Mincio and begins Bianco di Custoza DOC production.

wines.description: Corte Gardoni's Bianco di Custoza DOC from the morainic rim at Valeggio sul Mincio. A fresh, mineral Garganega-Trebbiano-Cortese blend for lake fish and casual Garda-region meals.

wines.name: Corte Gardoni Le Fontane Garda Cabernet DOC
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and Slavonian oak

taste.summary: The Le Fontane (the springs) red from Corte Gardoni is a Garda DOC Cabernet-based blend aged in oak. The Garda DOC permits Bordeaux varieties and the morainic soils of Valeggio sul Mincio give the Cabernet a mineral freshness that distinguishes it from warmer Veneto reds.

pairings.why: Herbal Cabernet notes echo the sage while firm structure handles the pork fat.
Dark fruit and firm tannins match well with the richness of duck and buckwheat polenta.

history.summary: Corte Gardoni introduced Bordeaux-variety reds in the late 1980s, taking advantage of the Garda DOC's allowance of international varieties. Named for the natural springs (fontane) on the estate, Le Fontane Cabernet represents the estate's attempt to produce a serious red alongside the lighter Bardolino Classico.

milestones.event: Corte Gardoni launches Le Fontane Cabernet as a Garda DOC oak-aged red.

wines.description: Corte Gardoni's Garda DOC Cabernet from morainic vineyards at Valeggio sul Mincio. Le Fontane blends Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot for a structured, herbal red with mineral freshness.

wines.name: Corte Gardoni Bardolino Classico DOC
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel for freshness

taste.summary: Corte Gardoni's Bardolino Classico is produced from Corvina-dominated vines on the southern Garda morainic soils. Light-bodied, pale ruby and fresh: a classic everyday Garda red suited to fish, vegetable dishes and outdoor drinking.

pairings.dish: Risotto al Bardolino (risotto made with Bardolino)
pairings.why: Regional tradition: the same wine in the risotto and in the glass.

pairings.dish: Carne salada di manzo (raw cured Garda beef)
pairings.why: Bardolino Classico is the traditional Garda accompaniment for thinly sliced, lemon-dressed cured beef.

history.summary: Corte Gardoni produces Bardolino Classico from the estate's Corvina and Rondinella vineyards on the morainic hills near the lake. The Classico sub-zone of the Bardolino DOC covers the historic production core around the lakefront village. The light ruby, sour-cherry style of Bardolino Classico is designed for immediate enjoyment.

milestones.event: Corte Gardoni begins Bardolino Classico DOC production from Corvina vines on the morainic rim.

wines.description: Corte Gardoni's Bardolino Classico DOC. Light ruby with sour cherry, rose petal and fresh herbs from Corvina vines on the morainic Garda shoreline; a refreshing everyday red.

wines.name: Cantina di Custoza Bianco di Custoza DOC
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel

taste.summary: The cooperative's flagship Bianco di Custoza is the appellation's most accessible, high-volume expression. Consistent, clean and approachable, it represents good-value Custoza DOC at the walk-in tourist tier. The five-variety blend from cooperative members' morainic vineyards gives a typical zonal character.

pairings.why: Clean and neutral, suitable as an everyday match for any simply prepared fish.
Accessible volume Custoza for the tourist-restaurant aperitivo circuit around Lake Garda.

history.summary: Cantina di Custoza was established in 1958 as the cooperative of local growers south of Lake Garda. It became the largest producer in the Bianco di Custoza DOC appellation at the DOC's foundation in 1971. The cooperative represents hundreds of grower families across the morainic hills.

milestones.event: Cantina di Custoza cooperative founded.
Bianco di Custoza DOC established; Cantina di Custoza becomes the largest single producer.

wines.description: The Custoza cooperative's accessible Bianco di Custoza DOC. Clean, light and consistent; a five-variety morainic blend at the volume tourist tier of the Lake Garda white wine market.

wines.name: Cantina di Custoza La Selva Bianco di Custoza DOC
wines.oak_regime: Partial fermentation in used oak barriques; majority stainless steel

taste.summary: La Selva is the cooperative's premium single-vineyard selection from older vines in the Selva parcel. Partial oak fermentation adds texture and complexity beyond the standard cooperative Custoza, demonstrating the appellation's premium potential.

pairings.why: The wine's cream texture and stone fruit complement roasted poultry.
Textured Custoza stands up to the brininess of bottarga and prawn pasta.

history.summary: La Selva was introduced by the Cantina di Custoza cooperative to demonstrate quality beyond the volume entry-level. Selected from older vines in the Selva sub-parcel of the cooperative's morainic vineyards, partial oak fermentation was chosen to show that Custoza DOC could produce structured, food-serious whites.

milestones.event: Cantina di Custoza introduces La Selva as a premium single-parcel oak-fermented tier.

wines.description: Cantina di Custoza's premium selection from the La Selva parcel. Partial oak fermentation adds cream texture and stone-fruit depth to the standard Bianco di Custoza DOC cooperative framework.

wines.name: Cantine Campagnola Bardolino Classico DOC
wines.oak_regime: Short period in Slavonian oak; primarily stainless steel

taste.summary: One of Bardolino's oldest estates, founded in 1889. Campagnola's Bardolino Classico is a genuine expression of the pale ruby, low-tannin, cherry-scented style from the original lakefront Bardolino zone. The estate's daily opening policy makes it a popular stop on the Lake Garda wine tourist circuit.

pairings.why: The quintessential Garda pairing: light Bardolino with freshwater lake fish.
Low tannin and cherry fruit work gently with the sweetness of fig and salt of ham.
Simple, fresh ingredients are elevated by a light red with good acidity.

history.summary: Cantine Campagnola was founded in 1889 at the Bardolino waterfront, making it one of the oldest continuously operating estates in the appellation. The family has maintained production of traditional Bardolino Classico using Corvina, Rondinella and Molinara from lakefront vineyards across six generations.

milestones.event: Campagnola family founds the Bardolino estate on the Lake Garda waterfront.
Bardolino Superiore elevated to DOCG; Campagnola continues Classico DOC production.

wines.description: Cantine Campagnola's benchmark Bardolino Classico from a 130-year-old Lake Garda estate. Light ruby, sour cherry and herbs; the historic lakefront Bardolino style for fish and casual meals.

wines.name: Cantine Campagnola Valpolicella Classico DOC
wines.oak_regime: 6 months in Slavonian oak botti

taste.summary: Campagnola's Valpolicella Classico from the estate's hillside vineyards in the historic Classico zone. A traditional expression of the DOC: light in colour and body but with the characteristic Corvina sour cherry and almond character of the Classico hills.

pairings.dish: Pasta e fagioli (pasta and bean soup)
pairings.why: Light Valpolicella with the classic bean soup is one of Verona's most traditional combinations.
The wine's earthy, herbal notes complement mushrooms and toasted polenta.
Tart cherry and almond bitterness are natural partners for air-dried salami.

history.summary: Campagnola expanded from Bardolino into the Valpolicella Classico zone in the early 20th century, acquiring hillside vineyards on the Classico slopes around Negrar and Sant'Ambrogio. The Valpolicella Classico label represents the lower tier of the estate's Valpolicella range, sitting below any Ripasso or Amarone production.

milestones.event: Campagnola acquires Valpolicella Classico hillside vineyards and begins Valpolicella DOC production.

wines.description: Campagnola's Valpolicella Classico DOC from hillside vineyards in the historic Classico zone. Traditional sour cherry, almond and herb character in a light, everyday Corvina blend.

wines.name: Cantine Campagnola Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
wines.oak_regime: 2-3 years in large Slavonian oak botti

taste.summary: Campagnola's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico is produced from appassimento-dried Corvina, Corvinone and Rondinella grapes from the Classico hills. Two to three years in large Slavonian oak develop the characteristic dried fruit, dark chocolate and tobacco complexity of the DOCG's flagship wine.

pairings.why: The traditional Veronese dish braised in Amarone; using the same wine in the glass is the regional custom.
Crystalline aged Parmesan and the wine's dried-fruit intensity create a balance of richness and structure.
The game's intensity requires Amarone's full body and structured tannins.

history.summary: Campagnola began Amarone production in the late 1960s when the wine was becoming commercially recognised beyond Italy. Though best known for Bardolino, the Campagnola estate's Classico hillside vineyards provide the Corvina and Corvinone for a traditional appassimento Amarone. The Campagnola version is positioned as an accessible quality Classico DOCG rather than a prestige collector wine.

milestones.event: Campagnola first produces Amarone della Valpolicella Classico for commercial release.
Amarone della Valpolicella elevated to DOCG.

wines.description: Cantine Campagnola's traditional Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG. Appassimento-dried Corvina and Corvinone aged 2-3 years in large oak; dried cherry, dark chocolate and tobacco in an accessible quality Classico tier.

wines.name: Monte Tondo Soave Superiore Casette Foscarin
wines.oak_regime: No oak; extended lees contact in stainless steel

taste.summary: Monte Tondo's signature Soave Superiore DOCG from the Casette Foscarin single-vineyard site on high-elevation basalt terraces east of the Classico zone. Extended lees contact adds texture while preserving the natural mineral character of volcanic Garganega.

pairings.why: The wine's almond-mineral profile is a classic counterpart to this Venetian salt cod specialty.

pairings.dish: Grilled sea bass with herb oil
pairings.why: Mineral and citrus-accented Soave Superiore is ideal with herb-dressed grilled sea bass.
Regional hard cheese from the Lessini mountains pairs naturally with the area's Soave Superiore.

history.summary: The Magnabosco family's Monte Tondo estate east of the Soave Classico zone has farmed the Casette Foscarin parcel on high-altitude basalt terraces since the 1960s. The single-vineyard bottling was introduced in 1988 to highlight the volcanic basalt and garnet-clay soils that produce a distinctly mineral expression of Garganega, distinct from the tufa-volcanic Classico zone.

milestones.event: Monte Tondo first releases Casette Foscarin as a single-vineyard Soave Superiore from high basalt terraces.

wines.description: Monte Tondo's single-vineyard Soave Superiore DOCG from high basalt terraces at Casette Foscarin. Mineral Garganega with white peach and almond from extended lees contact in stainless steel.

wines.name: Monte Tondo Soave Classico DOC
wines.oak_regime: No oak; stainless steel cold fermentation

taste.summary: Monte Tondo's entry-level Soave Classico DOC from vineyards within the historic Classico zone. A clean, accessible Garganega-Trebbiano blend for everyday drinking; lighter than the Casette Foscarin Superiore.

pairings.why: Classic pairing: clean, crisp Soave with lightly fried prawns.

pairings.dish: Cold pasta with tuna and olives
pairings.why: Versatile dry white for summer cold pasta preparations.

history.summary: Monte Tondo was founded in 1969 by the Magnabosco family on the volcanic hills east of the Soave Classico zone. The standard Soave Classico DOC represents the accessible entry level of the estate, drawing from vineyards within the historic Classico denomination.

milestones.event: Monte Tondo founded; first Soave Classico DOC production from the Magnabosco family.

wines.description: Monte Tondo's entry-level Soave Classico DOC. A clean Garganega-Trebbiano blend from the Classico zone with white flower, lemon and mineral freshness.

wines.name: Monte Tondo Miele Recioto di Soave DOCG
wines.oak_regime: 6 months in small oak; majority stainless steel

taste.summary: Miele (honey) is Monte Tondo's Recioto di Soave DOCG, a rare sweet wine from appassimento-dried Garganega grapes. Recioto di Soave predates Soave's dry style and is considered the original historic wine of the Soave hills. Monte Tondo's version captures honey, dried apricot and candied citrus balanced by the grape's naturally high acidity.

pairings.why: Classic Italian vino da dessert and cantucci; the wine's almond-honey echoes the biscuit.
Honey and apple character in both the wine and the traditional Veneto cake create a seamless match.
Sweet wine and young Grana Padano with a drizzle of acacia honey is a Veneto specialty pairing.

history.summary: Recioto di Soave is the ancient sweet wine of the Soave hills, predating the modern dry style by centuries. The DOCG was established in 1998 to protect and recognise this traditional wine. Monte Tondo's Miele Recioto from dried Garganega grapes represents the Magnabosco family's commitment to the historic wines of their zone.

milestones.event: Recioto di Soave elevated to DOCG.
Monte Tondo releases first Miele as a dedicated Recioto di Soave DOCG.

wines.description: Monte Tondo's Recioto di Soave DOCG from appassimento Garganega. Miele (honey) captures the ancient sweet wine tradition of the Soave hills: honey, dried apricot and candied citrus balanced by high acidity.

wines.name: Fasoli Gino Soave Classico Mael
wines.oak_regime: No oak; certified organic stainless steel cold fermentation

taste.summary: Mael is the flagship Soave Classico from Fasoli Gino, one of the Soave zone's pioneering certified organic estates. ICEA-certified organic viticulture at Colognola ai Colli on volcanic basalt soils gives this Garganega a particularly expressive mineral and orchard-fruit profile.

pairings.why: The wine's citrus acidity and mineral cut through the agrodolce brine of this classic Venetian preparation.
Mineral Soave and clam pasta is one of the Veneto's defining wine-and-pasta pairings.
Young Monte Veronese from the Lessini mountains is the regional pairing for Soave Classico.

history.summary: Fasoli Gino pioneered organic viticulture in the Soave zone, converting to ICEA-certified organic farming in the late 1980s at their Colognola ai Colli estate. The Mael cuvee (named for a local place name) was developed as the flagship expression of the estate's certified-organic Garganega production from volcanic basalt soils.

milestones.event: Fasoli Gino releases Mael as the flagship Soave Classico DOC from the estate's volcanic parcels.
Fasoli Gino converts to ICEA-certified organic viticulture, among the first in the Soave zone.

wines.description: Fasoli Gino's ICEA-certified organic Soave Classico DOC flagship. Mael is grown on volcanic basalt at Colognola ai Colli; mineral Garganega with white peach, almond and crisp acidity.

verified.address_quoted: Via C. Battisti 47, 37030 Colognola ai Colli (VR)

wines.name: Fasoli Gino Le Bine Soave Superiore DOCG
wines.oak_regime: No oak; extended lees contact in stainless steel

taste.summary: Le Bine is the top single-vineyard Soave Superiore DOCG from Fasoli Gino's organically farmed estate. 'Bine' are the traditional wooden stakes used for old Garganega training; this label celebrates the estate's old-vine heritage from individual pergola-trained parcels on volcanic basalt.

pairings.why: Textured, mineral Soave Superiore is ideal with a richer asparagus-and-prawn risotto.

pairings.dish: Whole baked sea bream with capers
pairings.why: Mineral stone-fruit complexity matches the brininess of capers and whole baked fish.

history.summary: Le Bine was introduced to represent Fasoli Gino's oldest organic Garganega vines within the Soave Superiore DOCG zone. The name references the traditional wooden vine-training stakes (bine) used for pergola-trained Garganega, a training system being progressively replaced by Guyot in the Soave zone. Le Bine is a tribute to the old method.

milestones.event: Fasoli Gino introduces Le Bine as a single-old-vine-parcel Soave Superiore DOCG from certified organic Garganega.

wines.description: Fasoli Gino's old-vine single-parcel Soave Superiore DOCG. Le Bine celebrates pergola-trained organic Garganega on volcanic basalt; textured stone fruit, hazelnut and long mineral complexity.

verified.address_quoted: Via C. Battisti 47, 37030 Colognola ai Colli (VR)

wines.name: Fasoli Gino San Zeno Recioto di Soave DOCG
wines.oak_regime: 6 months in small French oak; partial; majority stainless steel

taste.summary: San Zeno is Fasoli Gino's Recioto di Soave DOCG, made from dried organic Garganega grapes. Organic farming preserves the grape's natural concentration and flavour integrity through drying. The result is a rich, golden sweet wine of exceptional aromatic purity.

pairings.why: The classic Venetian pairing: regional sweet wine with the regional corn biscuit.
Honey and caramel notes in both the wine and the dessert create an indulgent match.

history.summary: San Zeno is named for the medieval Basilica di San Zeno in Verona, which historically maintained close ties to Soave viticulture. Fasoli Gino's organic Recioto is distinguished from conventional versions by the absence of systemic treatments during the appassimento period, resulting in a purer expression of Garganega's natural sweetness.

milestones.event: Fasoli Gino releases San Zeno Recioto as a certified organic Recioto di Soave DOCG.

wines.description: Fasoli Gino's certified organic Recioto di Soave DOCG. San Zeno uses ICEA-certified organic dried Garganega for a pure honey, apricot and almond sweet wine with exceptional aromatic integrity.

verified.address_quoted: Via C. Battisti 47, 37030 Colognola ai Colli (VR)

wines.name: Nino Franco Brut Nature Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore
wines.oak_regime: No oak; zero-dosage Charmat method

taste.summary: Nino Franco's zero-dosage Brut Nature expression for consumers wanting the driest, most mineral face of Valdobbiadene DOCG Glera. Under 3 g/l residual sugar and racy acidity make this one of the most food-focused Proseccos in the portfolio.

pairings.why: Zero dosage and racy mineral acidity are ideal for a mixed seafood platter.
The austerity of Brut Nature is lifted by the lactic richness of burrata.

history.summary: The Brut Nature category emerged in the Prosecco DOCG zone in the 2010s as producers responded to demand for drier, more artisanal expressions. Nino Franco's Brut Nature represents Primo Franco's commitment to showing the full range of Glera's expression, from the classic fruity Rustico to the austerity of zero dosage.

milestones.event: Nino Franco releases a dedicated Brut Nature (zero dosage) Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG.

wines.description: Nino Franco's zero-dosage Brut Nature Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG. Racy acidity and chalk mineral in the austere, dry end of the Glera spectrum.

wines.name: Bisol Jeio Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Brut
wines.oak_regime: No oak; standard Charmat method

taste.summary: Jeio is the accessible everyday Brut tier from Bisol 1542, positioned below the premium Crede multi-varietal and the Rive expressions. The Glera-dominant blend with minor Verdiso and Bianchetta adds a touch of aromatic complexity to a clean, food-friendly Brut.

pairings.why: Light, clean Brut Prosecco Superiore is the universal aperitivo at any Venetian bacaro.
The local white asparagus from the Bassano del Grappa area pairs naturally with Valdobbiadene DOCG Brut.

history.summary: Jeio was introduced to offer a lighter, more casual entry point to the Bisol range than the flagship Crede. Priced slightly below Crede, Jeio uses fewer secondary varietals and a standard Charmat contact time for a simpler but consistent Valdobbiadene DOCG Brut expression.

milestones.event: Bisol introduces Jeio as an accessible Brut below the flagship Crede multi-varietal.

wines.description: Bisol 1542's accessible everyday Brut Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG. Jeio blends Glera with minor Verdiso and Bianchetta for a clean, crisp aperitivo sparkling.
